I’m out of position, so I don’t want to inflate the pot with a marginal hand by raising — a raise would commit chips OOP against many hands that currently dominate me (Tx, 66, overpairs). Plan: call the small probe, see a turn and re-evaluate; fold to large, polarizing turn barrels, and continue versus small/medium turn bets to realize my equity.

Check. I'm out of position with Qh9d on T♠-6♦-6♠-2♣ — no pair and minimal showdown value. I called a small flop probe to realize equity, but I don't want to inflate the pot OOP with a marginal hand by betting the turn. Flash's line (small flop c-bet) is consistent with a wide range that includes tens, pocket sixes, overpairs, and many hands that either beat me or will call a bluff. Checking controls the pot size, preserves fold equity on later streets, and lets me see a free river if he also checks. Plan: check; call small/medium river bets to realize equity; fold to large, polarizing bets. If he checks back the turn I'll reassess on the river and only bluff/lead when the card and blockers justify it.

The turn card (2c) is a complete brick that doesn't change the board state significantly. Minni's call on the flop suggests she likely has a piece of the board, such as a Ten, a pocket pair, or a spade draw. My King-high has almost no equity to improve and it is unlikely she will fold a Ten or a Six to a single turn bet. By checking back, I control the size of the pot and get to see the river for free. I can then decide whether to give up or attempt a bluff on the river depending on the card and her action.

Board T♠-6♦-6♠-2♣-3♥, I only have Q♥9♦ — queen-high with no pair and almost no river-improving cards. Flash bet a tiny flop and then checked the turn, a line consistent with either a ten, pocket sixes, an overpair or simply pot-control with medium strength; he also checks a lot of bluffs on the turn. Pot is ~1100 and there is no bet on the river, so betting here would be a thin bluff that often runs into hands that currently beat me (T/6/overpairs) and won’t get enough folds from hands I beat. I’ll check to realize showdown and avoid bloating the pot with a marginal hand. If faced with a small river bet I’d call to get to showdown; fold to a large, polarized shove.

On the river with a board of Ts-6d-6s-2c-3h, I hold King-high. The action has been relatively passive: I made a small continuation bet on the flop which Minni called, followed by both of us checking the turn and now Minni checking the river. The board is very dry, and Minni's line (Check-Call, Check, Check) often represents a weak pocket pair, a Ten with a poor kicker, or occasionally a missed high-card float like AJ/AQ. While a bet might fold out some better hands (like Ace-high or small pairs), my King-high has a small amount of showdown value against her missed draws or lower broadway cards. Given Minni's large stack, she may be inclined to call a 'frustration' bet with any Ace or even a King-high with a better kicker. Checking back is the safest play to realize my equity without risking more chips against a player who can afford to call light.

3-handed on the button with KQs suited, a premium broadway hand with great playability. First to act preflop, standard to raise for value and to thin the field. Opponents have large stacks (Minni very deep), so a standard 3x BB raise to 300 builds the pot without overcommitting. Minni and Flash have shown aggression but also folding tendencies to raises. This hand dominates most calling ranges and can make straights/flushes.

5-2 offsuit is one of the weakest hands — out of position in the small blind after a button open. Pot is 450 and I must call 250; that requires ~35.7% equity (250/(450+250)) just to break even. Versus a button opening range my equity with 5d2h is far below that, and the big blind can still enter making it likely a multiway pot where this hand performs even worse. I have a large stack and should preserve chips for better spots rather than defend with a junk hand. Fold.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Jack offsuit, a premium hand in a 3-handed game. Elon has raised from the Button, which usually represents a wide range of hands. With a stack of 7900, I have plenty of room to 3-bet and take control of the hand. 3-betting to 900 puts pressure on Elon's 4575-chip stack and may win the pot immediately. If he calls, I have a strong hand to play post-flop with the initiative.

Facing a 3-bet from Flash in the BB after my button open-raise to 300. KQs suited is a premium hand 3-handed with excellent playability, position advantage postflop, and good equity against typical BB 3-bet ranges (pairs, AK, broadways). Pot odds are excellent (600 to call into 1250, ~4.2:1), and calling keeps the pot manageable while realizing my hand's potential IP. Folding is too tight 3-handed; 4-betting risks commitment with a hand that plays better multi-street OOP for opponent. Flash has been aggressive but folds to pressure sometimes; postflop dynamics favor me.

I am the pre-flop aggressor after 3-betting from the Big Blind with Ace-Jack offsuit. The flop of 5c-2c-6d is relatively low and doesn't hit my perceived range strongly, but it also likely missed much of Elon's button-calling range, though he could have mid-pairs or suited connectors. I have two overcards and a backdoor club flush draw with the Jack of Clubs. Leading with a continuation bet of 750 (approximately 40% of the pot) maintains my initiative and puts pressure on Elon's remaining stack (3975). Given the low SPR (2.15), this bet size allows me to fold if he shoves and I feel behind, or continue if the price is right on later streets.

3-handed on button with KQs suited after calling a BB 3-bet preflop. Flop 5c2c6d is a wet, connected board with a flush draw. I have complete air: king-high overcards and a weak backdoor spade draw, no pair, no straight draw, no flush draw. Flash's preflop 3-bet and flop bet of 750 into ~1800 pot (~40%) represents strength: sets (22,55,66), two-pair combos (e.g., 53s, 25s suited connectors), flush draws (esp. club suited), pair+draws. My equity is crushed against this range (~20-30% at best). Pot odds ~3.5:1 but implied odds poor (no made hand potential, reverse implied disaster). Raising as a bluff is risky OOP opponent who showed aggression (e.g., recent hands 60,61 where Flash 3-bets/raises), deep effective stacks but my 3975 commits too much (~20%). Better to fold and preserve stack for better spots; position allows stealing limps/folds elsewhere. Disciplined fold.

5♦8♠ is a weak, non‑connecting offsuit hand but I’m on the button with both blinds posted and excellent positional advantage postflop. The cost to see a flop is only 100 into a 150 pot — good pot odds to realize equity and play the hand in position. A raise here with this holding is unnecessary: it bloats the pot, risk‑isolates me against Elon’s shorter stack, and invites 3‑bets. Plan: call, see the flop, and proceed cautiously — continue with good draws or pairs, fold to heavy aggression when not improving.

I am in the Small Blind with Qd 7c. Minni has limped from the button. The pot is 250 and it only costs me 50 more to call, giving me 5-to-1 odds to see a flop. While Q7o is not a strong hand, it has enough equity against a button limping range to justify completing the small blind, provided I play cautiously post-flop. Elon in the Big Blind is relatively short-stacked, so I must be prepared for a potential squeeze, but for now, the price to call is too good to pass up.

Preflop in BB with QJo facing limps from SB (Flash, 88BB) and BTN (Minni, deep stack). QJo is a strong hand (~top 12% equity), plays well multiway but better heads-up. Limpers likely have wide, weak ranges. Raising builds a pot with my strong hand, takes initiative OOP, and charges their speculative hands. Standard size: pot is 300, raising to 600 total (500 more) is ~2x pot, balanced aggression without overcommitting my 39BB stack. If called/3bet, can reassess postflop playability.

5♦8♠ is a weak, non‑connecting offsuit hand, but I'm on the button with a large positional advantage and a very deep stack relative to Elon. Elon raised to 600 from the big blind (500 more for me) and Flash in the small blind has already called; calling keeps the pot controllable, preserves my post‑flop positional edge, and gives me good odds to see a flop and realize equity. A raise to 1100 would unnecessarily bloat the pot and isolate me against a short‑stack who can shove; I don't want to commit chips with a marginal hand preflop. Plan: call and evaluate the flop — proceed aggressively with pairs or clear draws, and check/fold to heavy aggression when I miss. If Elon jams all‑in, I will re‑evaluate based on his shove frequency and my implied equity, but I won't voluntarily inflate the pot here.

I am in the Small Blind with Queen-Seven offsuit. While I initially completed the small blind for a small amount, the situation has changed significantly with Elon's raise to 600 and Minni's call. Q7o is a weak hand that performs poorly in multi-way pots, especially when played out of position. Elon's stack is relatively short, which suggests his raising range from the Big Blind is likely strong or at least contains hands that dominate mine. Given the lack of coordination and equity this hand has against a raising range, calling 500 more is a negative expectation play. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity to commit chips.

Flop of 6s-8d-3h completely misses my QcJs – queen-high air with no pair, draw, or even backdoor potential. Board is dry rainbow, favoring paired ranges. Preflop, Minni limped BTN (wide range) and called my 3x raise in position, so her range includes broadway, suited connectors (like 45,67,79, etc.), Ax suited, pockets, but OOP continuation bet as a bluff with total air risks too much equity loss. Pot 1300, my stack 3375 (~34BB effective vs her deep stack). Standard check OOP to pot control, realize equity if she checks back, or fold to her bet without committing further. Bluffing here polarizes my range too much early in tournament; better spots exist. Checking induces her bluffs or keeps pot small.

I have top pair (8s) with a weak 5 kicker on a dry 6♠-8♦-3♥ board. Elon checked, which can be air or a pot-control line with a medium/strong hand — either way this is a spot to take the initiative and extract value while denying free cards. The pot is 1,300 and Elon is short (≈3,375), so a reasonably-sized bet will get called by worse hands (A/x, smaller pairs, broadways) or induce a shove that I can call comfortably. I don't want to bloat the pot unnecessarily, but I want a size that gets value and puts pressure on his decision. Betting ~900 (~2/3 pot) achieves that: it charges worse, keeps fold equity, and leaves me room to call a shove without risking a large portion of my stack. Plan: bet for value now; if he jams I will call for stack given my top pair vs his likely shove range.

Flop completely misses my QJo: queen-high with no pair, no draws, and minimal backdoor equity on this dry 6s-8d-3h rainbow board. Preflop, Minni's BTN limp-call to my isolation raise suggests a wide but speculative range including suited connectors, small pairs, broadway cards, and Ax suited. Her 900 bet into 2200 (~40% pot) after my check indicates strength: likely pairs (66/88/33), overpairs, or bluffs with total air/gutshots, but her position and initiative favor value-heavy. Pot odds require ~22% equity for a call, but QJo has near-zero equity against her polarized betting range (crushed by pairs+, folds too infrequently). Stack depth (34BB effective) demands discipline; calling risks 25%+ of stack OOP with airball, bloating pot for turns where I'm still behind. Folding preserves stack for premium spots in this early tournament stage. Check-fold is standard GTO play here to avoid spew.

On the button in a three-handed game, King-Nine offsuit is a strong enough hand to open for a raise. I want to put pressure on the blinds and take control of the pot. Given Elon's relatively short stack (33BB), a standard 3x raise is effective while still allowing me to fold if he decide to shove and I feel I'm behind.

62o is one of the worst possible hands in poker, with terrible playability, no high card strength, and offsuit. In the small blind facing a button raise to 3x, the pot odds to call 250 into 450 are about 2:1, but my equity against a typical button opening range is abysmal (~20-25% at best vs. villain's range). Minni in BB has a massive stack (276x BB) and is likely to 3-bet or defend wide. My effective stack is only 33BB, but no need to speculate with trash. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities. History shows Flash is aggressive on button, but discipline matters.

Flash opened to 300 from the button — a wide, aggressive opening range. Q♦J♦ is a strong, playable hand in the big blind with good equity and post‑flop playability. I want to take the initiative, get value from worse Broadway hands and suited connectors, and apply pressure to his wide BTN range, so I 3‑bet. I choose a standard 3‑bet to 900 (≈3x the open): large enough to gain fold equity and extract value but not so large as to overcommit given deep stacks. Plan: if called, continue with CBets on favorable textures and pot control on dangerous ace‑high/raggy boards; if Flash 4‑bets very large representing a polarized range, I will fold and preserve the deep stack.

I opened King-Nine offsuit from the button, which is a standard play in a three-handed game. However, Minni in the Big Blind has responded with a 3-bet to 900. While K9o has some value three-handed, it performs poorly against a 3-betting range, especially from a chip leader who has shown significant aggression. Calling puts me in a spot where I'm likely dominated or folding on most flops, and 4-betting is too risky against Minni's massive stack. Given that Elon is relatively short-stacked, I can afford to wait for better opportunities rather than risking chips in a 3-bet pot with a marginal hand.

Td7c is a weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity and playability post-flop. Even on the button in what appears to be a limped pot (pot 150, to call 100 likely SB limp + BB), this hand lacks value or speculative potential worth investing 100 chips. My stack is 33BB effective, but with deep opponents, better to preserve chips for premium spots rather than enter pots with marginal holdings preflop. Standard fold to maximize long-term EV.

Heads-up from the small blind with pocket deuces and deep stacks (28k vs Flash 8.45k). This is a great spot to take the initiative: pocket 2s have strong implied odds for set-mining and also get decent fold equity heads-up. Standard open-sizing at this table is ~3x BB, so I'll raise to 300 total to apply pressure, build a pot if called, and keep my range strong. If Flash folds I scoop the 150 pot; if called I plan to see the flop and look to hit a set or use position dynamics postflop. I will re-evaluate if faced with a large 3-bet (fold to very large 3-bets that price me poorly, consider calling smaller 3-bets to set-mine).

I am in the Big Blind with 3h 6c, which is a very weak hand. Minni, the chip leader, has raised from the Small Blind. While I have 2:1 odds to call, this hand is extremely difficult to play out of position post-flop, and I would likely be throwing away chips against an aggressive opponent. I will fold and wait for a better spot to utilize my stack.

I'm on the button with 5s6c in a 3-handed pot. In 3-handed play the button should open very wide — 5‑6 off is marginal but playable in position. I will raise to 300 (a standard ~3x BB open here) to seize the initiative, apply pressure to the blinds and deny a free flop. Flash (SB) is likely to call fairly wide; Elon (BB) is relatively short, so this sizing is enough to isolate without committing my deep stack. Plan: if I take the lead I'll c-bet favorable flops and play cautiously when called (check/lead on very dry boards, continue on draws and strong pairs). If I face a shove or large 3‑bet from a tight opponent I'll fold this hand — only call an all‑in if the shove range is extremely wide and pot odds justify it. Overall an open to 300 is the +EV, position-exploiting line.

I am holding 6d 3s in the small blind, which is one of the weakest starting hands in poker. Minni has raised from the button, and I have Elon, a short stack, behind me in the big blind. Calling would put me out of position against the chip leader with a hand that has very little equity or post-flop playability. Given my stack size and the strength of my hand, folding is the only sensible play here.

KJ offsuit is a strong enough hand to defend the big blind against a button raise after the small blind folds. Minni's button raise is likely wide given position and history of aggression. Pot odds are excellent: calling an additional 200 into a 450 pot requires only about 18% equity, which KJ crushes. Postflop playability is good with straight potential and king high. Raising risks bloating the pot OOP against a deep-stacked opponent who calls frequently. Prefer calling to control pot size and realize equity.

Preflop call with KJ offsuit from BB vs button raise was standard for pot odds and playability. Flop 7c-3h-5d is a dry, disconnected board that misses most of my calling range but favors my king-high showdown value against Minni's wide button opening range, which includes many Ax, Kx, Qx, suited connectors, and bluffs. As OOP first to act, leading out risks getting raised off equity or check-raised; better to check and pot control, inducing bluffs or value from worse while realizing equity on a board where Minni c-bets frequently (per history: aggressive button play in hands like 53,56,62). Pot odds post-bet will be favorable given SPR (~4.6:1), allowing check-call small cbets or check-fold larger ones. No strong draws or made hands justify aggression OOP. Checking keeps pot small and exploits Minni's tendency to bet wide/light.

I opened preflop and the flop (7c 3h 5d) gives me a pair of fives plus a small straight draw (a 4 makes 3-4-5-6-7). Elon defended preflop with a wide range and his check here is not convincing strength. This is a dry board that’s good for a continuation bet to extract value from worse pairs/overcards and to charge any backdoor draws while keeping the pot manageable. I’ll make a modest c-bet (~1/3–1/2 pot) to get value and deny free cards: bet 250. If he check-raises big I’ll give it up; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and proceed cautiously.

Facing a small 250 bet into 900 on a dry 7c-3h-5d flop after checking as OOP. KJo is king-high with no pair or draw but strong blockers (Kc blocks her Kx) and good showdown equity against Minni's wide button c-bet range, which includes Ax, Qx, offsuit broadways, suited gappers, and air (consistent with her aggressive history: frequent small bets on flops like in hands 53,56,65,68). Pot odds are excellent: 250 to call into 900 (pot becomes 1150) requires ~18% equity, and KJo has ~30-40% vs her polarized range. Folding squanders equity OOP on a board that favors my high cards over her bluffs/overcards. Raising is suboptimal: low fold equity against calling station tendencies (e.g., Hand 47 called big bets), bloats pot OOP with marginal hand, vulnerable to 4-bet shoves. Check-call small bet realizes equity, keeps SPR low (~2.4 post-call), controls pot, induces further bluffs on turn if missed. Consistent with preflop/flop plan to pot control and exploit her aggression profitably while preserving short stack (30BB).

Preflop and flop actions were correct: calling wide button raise OOP with KJo for pot odds and playability, then check-calling small flop c-bet (~18% odds) to realize king-high showdown value against Minni's aggressive, wide button range (Ax, broadways, suited gappers, bluffs per history: small bets in 53,56,62,65). Turn 3c pairs the deuce-free flop, slightly favors value hands but improves few draws (now flush draw possible with clubs, but blocks her Kc); my Kc Jh retains strong blockers (K blocks top pair, c blocker) and ~25-35% equity vs her continuing range, which remains polarized/cappy after flop call (overpairs, sets unlikely given board, more TP/TK, floats, missed draws). OOP first with 2775 stack (~28BB) into 1150 pot (SPR ~2.4), betting risks bloating pot with marginal showdown hand vulnerable to check-raise (Minni check-raises occasionally, e.g., 35,68) and low fold equity vs her calling station traits (Hand 47,37). Leading thin value suboptimal on paired board where she can have 3x. Checking controls pot, induces bluffs/value bets from worse (QJ, AJ, bluffs), sets up favorable river SPR/call-down if she bets small/light (her style). Consistent with pot control strategy to exploit aggression profitably long-term. Fold equity unnecessary; realize equity check.

Check for pot control. I c-bet the flop and Elon called; the turn (3c) paired the board and turned my 5s into a marginal second pair with only a thin 4-out straight backdoor. That card also gives him clear trip-3 combos (A3/K3/… ) that now beat me, and betting here risks bloating the pot or getting shoved on by better hands. I’ll check to keep the pot manageable, induce bluffs, and only call small bets; I’ll re-evaluate (and be willing to fold) if he jams with a very strong line.

Preflop and prior streets played correctly with pot control using KJo OOP vs Minni's wide button range: called pre for odds/playability, check-called small flop c-bet (~18% odds) realizing K-high equity vs polarized c-bet range (Ax, broadways, bluffs per her aggressive history e.g., hands 53,56,62,65,68,71 flop small bet). Turn 3c pairs low board; check-check keeps SPR low (~2 post-turn), induces bluffs as she slows down (suggests capped range: no 3x/trips likely given check-behind, more Ax/Kx/Qx/floats/missed draws without strong 3 blocker). River Js pairs my Jh for top pair top kicker (TPTK) on 7c3h5d3cJs board (no flush/straight completed; J blocks her AJ/QJ/JT). Now ~35-45% equity vs her river range (beats all bluffs/A-high, many Qx/Tx/pairs below J; loses to sets/Jx/7x rarely). First to act into 1150 pot with 2775 stack (~28BB, SPR~2.4), value betting exploits her calling station tendencies (e.g., Hand 47 multi-street calls, Hand 37 calls big) and history of paying off light (Hand 51 river call). Bet 700 (~60% pot) sizes for value from worse (AQ/KQ/Ax/air), low fold equity needed but gets called by ~25% range we beat while protecting vs draws. Checking risks free showdown (she may check back worse) or getting outdrawn by bluffs she leads. Betting maximizes value long-term vs aggressive button who overcalls.

I have second pair (5♠) with a weak 6-kicker on 7♣–3♥–5♦–3♣–J♠. The pot is 1850 and Elon’s river bet is 700; calling 700 gives me ~27% pot odds (700 to win ~2550). Elon called my flop c-bet and checked the turn, then fires a relatively small river bet — a line consistent with thin value (Jx, A7/K7, A5, some 3x) and also missed overcards/bluffs. My hand has better than ~27% equity versus a mixed calling/bleeding range and will beat many of his bluffs and worse pairs, so a call to realize that equity is +EV. Raising would bloating the pot out of position with a marginal hand and risk a shove from his short stack; folding surrenders too much equity. I will call 700 and fold to a shove if he moves all‑in.

In 3-handed play, Q5s is a standard opening hand from the button to put pressure on the blinds. Raising allows me to take the initiative and potentially win the pot pre-flop. Elon is relatively short-stacked, so I need to be mindful of a shove, but overall this is a plus-EV open.

Hand is 2c6d, a complete trash hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or high card value. Facing a button raise from Flash, who opens wide but still crushes this hand. I'm OOP in SB with poor implied odds and low stack (4575). Pot odds are ~2.8:1 but equity is far below break-even vs any reasonable range. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

I’m in the big blind with A♦3♦ facing a button open to 300 (Elon folded). Pot is 450 and I need 200 more (300 total). With deep stacks and a wide button opening range, A3s is well within my defend range: it has nut-suit potential, an ace blocker, and good implied odds. A tiny reraise to 500 would buy little fold equity and still leave me out of position postflop; a larger 3-bet would bloat the pot with a marginal hand. I’ll call 200 more (300 total) to see a flop, keep the pot controllable, and play postflop — look to continue on A/diamond flops or realize equity otherwise.

Board J♠ 2♦ 7♣, I hold A♦3♦. I have Ace-high plus a weak backdoor diamond draw (need both turn+river), and importantly the A♦ is a strong blocker to opponent Ace-high/top-pair combos. Flash opened wide from the button preflop, so his range contains a lot of air and weak made hands that can fold to pressure. I called preflop specifically to continue on A/diamond flops, so I'll make a continuation-semi-bluff to take the initiative. A half-pot bet (~325 into 650) gives meaningful fold equity, keeps the pot controllable if called, and allows me to fold to big aggression (this hand is marginal OOP). Plan: bet 325. If he folds I take it down. If he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and generally call small bets to realize equity or fold to heavy raises unless I improve (pair an Ace/3 or hit runner-runner diamonds).

I opened Q5s from the button and was called by Minni in the big blind. The flop comes J-2-7 rainbow. Minni leads out with a donk-bet of 325 into a 650 pot. At this point, I have nothing but Queen-high and a backdoor diamond draw. While a donk-bet can sometimes indicate weakness or a marginal hand, Minni has shown a high level of aggression and a willingness to stay in pots with a wide range. With no pair and no significant draw on a relatively dry board, there is little reason to continue or attempt a bluff raise here. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

Preflop on the button with 2c3c suited connectors. Excellent position to play speculative hands like this due to implied odds if multiway or fold equity against blinds. Button opening range is wide (~40-50% of hands), and 23s fits as a standard raise. Pot is 150, min raise to 200, but standard open is 2.5-3x BB to 250-300. Raising to 300 applies pressure: Minni (SB deepstack) may fold weak hands, Flash (BB shorter) defends wide but we have position. My stack ~46BB effective, plenty of room postflop. Folding too tight; limping bad practice. Aim to steal or play profitably.

Call 250. Qh8h is a hand I want to defend from the SB against a 3BB button open: it has good playability and suited-queen equity, and with my very deep stack the implied odds justify seeing a flop. I’m out of position to the opener and don’t want to turn this into a large isolation war with a marginal hand, so a min-3bet to 500 is suboptimal (too small to fold him out and too much commitment OOP). If the BB (Flash) calls we may be multiway, so I’ll play the flop cautiously: continue with top pair, strong hearts draws, or good equity; fold to heavy action when missed. If Elon or Flash 3-bets/raises significantly preflop I’ll re-evaluate based on pot odds and stack sizes.

K2o is a weak hand to play out of position in a multi-way pot. Elon raised from the button and Minni called from the small blind, suggesting they have stronger ranges than my current hand. While the price to call is only 200 more, K2o has very poor equity realization out of position and is easily dominated by other King-X hands. I will fold to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

I have Qh8h on 2h Js 7h — a very strong continuing hand: nut-ish heart draw plus an overcard to the J. That’s roughly 12 raw outs (9 hearts + 3 queens) and about ~45% to improve by the river, so this is a clear semi-bluff OOP. Action is checked to me; I want to take the initiative, get fold equity against Elon’s wide button-opening range, and build a pot when I have large equity. I also don’t want to overcommit OOP, so I’ll choose a half‑pot-ish c-bet to put pressure but keep the sizing controllable. Bet = 400 into a 700 pot gives folds from many weaker Jx/Tx/Qx hands and buys me favorable spots on the turn if called. Plan if faced with a large raise/all-in: re-evaluate — a shove likely represents a made hand and the pot odds won’t be attractive, so I will often fold this semi-bluff to heavy action; if he simply calls I proceed with the draw and re-assess on the turn.

Preflop raised 23c suited connectors from button for position and playability multiway. Flop 2h Jh 7h gives me bottom pair (2c) with a weak 3c kicker, backdoor club flush draw, no straight draw. Board has flush draw (two hearts) and possible straight draws, but position on SB is excellent. Minni leads 400 into 700 (57% pot), which could be Jx, 7x, flush draws, or thin value/A-high. My equity is decent (~30-40% vs her range) with position and implied odds against her deep stack (26k). Pot odds 1100-to-400 (~2.75:1, need ~27%). Calling keeps pot small, realizes equity, and allows turn control. Raising risks too weak a hand (vulnerable to better pairs/draws), folding too tight given position and board not hitting her range too hard. Plan: call, evaluate turn (fold if flush completes or strong bet, bet/fold if check). Stack 4275 effective plenty of room.

Turn: I hold Qh8h on 2h Js 7h 5h — I’ve turned a made queen‑high flush. Elon opened preflop and merely called my flop c‑bet, so his range contains lots of Jx, mid pairs and some single‑heart combos; very few combos (primarily Ah and maybe Kh hearts) legitimately beat me. This is a clear value bet. I’ll size to about 2/3 pot (1000 into 1500) — large enough to extract value from Jx, second pairs and smaller hearts, but not so large that I fold out most of his calling range or unnecessarily isolate to an all‑in. Plan on responses: if he jams, I’ll call — his shove will be capped by a shallow stack and I’m behind only to a small number of heart combinations; if he flats I’m still well ahead and will re‑evaluate river for more value; if he folds I win the pot now.

Facing a turn bet of 1000 into 1500 on a very draw-heavy board (2h Js 7h 5h, three hearts). My 2c3c gives bottom pair with a terrible kicker, no draws beyond backdoor clubs, and vulnerable to any Jx, 7x, 5x, or completed flush draws. Minni led flop small (likely wide range including draws/pairs), I called with position, but her larger turn bet (66% pot) indicates strength—value with top pair or better, or strong draw. Pot odds ~3.5:1 require ~22% equity, but my equity is likely <20% vs her polarized range (Jx/sets/flushes/draws). Position doesn't help enough post-river on this scary board; river bricks unlikely to improve me meaningfully. Committing another 1000 leaves me 2875 behind into 3500 pot (~28BB SPR), awkward spot without showdown value. Folding preserves stack for better spots; we've seen Minni capable of bluffing but this line looks value-heavy given board and sizing progression.

I’m on the button with J♠5♦ in a three-handed pot — good position and plenty of fold equity. Both blinds have middling stacks (Flash 79.5bb, Elon 37.8bb) and have shown a tendency to fold to aggression; Elon in particular has folded to pressure before. J5o is a marginal hand, but on the button it’s fully inside my opening range as a steal attempt and for postflop playability. I’ll raise to 300 (≈3x BB) to take the initiative: the size is big enough to generate meaningful fold equity and discourage light calls, but not so big that I commit too much with a marginal hand. Plan: fold to large 3-bets/shoves preflop; if called, use position to c-bet thin on dry, favorable boards and exercise pot control on coordinated boards — look to take the pot down or realize equity cheaply.

I am holding 2d 5c in the Small Blind. Minni has raised to 300 from the button. This is one of the weakest starting hands in poker and playing it out of position against a button raise is unprofitable. I will fold to preserve my stack.

Preflop in BB facing a button raise from Minni to 300 (200 to call into 450 pot). My hand Qc9h is offsuit but queen-high with decent equity against a wide button opening range, which Minni likely has given her aggressive history of frequent raises and continuation betting. Pot odds are excellent (~3.25:1), requiring only ~23% equity, and Q9o realizes well heads-up. Defending wide vs button is standard to deny fold equity and realize equity OOP. Folding too tight would be exploitable. No strong 3-bet value or bluff candidate here. Will call and play post-flop cautiously, using position disadvantage but leveraging Minni's aggression for traps or floats.

On this Ace-high rainbow flop (Td As Qh), I flop top pair with Qc9h but a weak 9-kicker, making my hand vulnerable to any Ax in Minni's wide button opening range, which likely includes AQ, AK, AT, AJ, and many overcards or draws. OOP as BB defender, betting would be suboptimal against her position and aggression, as it risks getting raised or played poorly postflop. Checking realizes equity by inducing bluffs/c-bets from her wide continuation range while controlling pot size. Pot odds will be favorable for a call against small bets (~33% pot), where I have ~40% equity vs her range; larger bets get folded to avoid cooler spots. Her history of frequent flops bets and button raises supports checking to exploit over-aggression without overcommitting stack (3575 effective). Long-term EV positive by defending wide preflop and playing selectively postflop.

Check. I opened preflop and Elon just called; the flop T♦ A♠ Q♥ heavily favors the caller’s defending range (broadways, Ax, middle pairs, Tx). With J♠5♦ I have no pair, only 4 clean outs to a Broadway straight (Ks) and a remote backdoor diamond possibility — very weak equity. C‑betting here will get called by too many better hands and can blow the pot up out of position against a shorter opponent who can pot‑commit. My earlier plan was to exercise pot control on coordinated/high boards, so I’ll check to keep the pot small, realize equity cheaply, and reassess on the turn (call small bets to see cards, fold to large aggression, or pursue a bluff if the turn gives a reasonable story or improves my hand).

On the turn 2h, still OOP with top pair Qc9h and a weak 9-kicker on an ace-high board (Td As Qh 2h). The 2h is a low blank but brings a backdoor heart flush draw completion and possible gutshots (e.g., KJ broadway still live, J9 straight). Minni's button check on flop after my check suggests possible weakness (missed Ax/draws/weak pairs) from her wide opening range, but she could trap Ax or slowplay strong hands. Betting thin value risks raising from Ax/KQ/AQ/better Qx, bloating pot OOP where my hand plays poorly. Checking maintains pot control, induces bluffs from her position/aggression (frequent in history), allows cheap showdown or fold to big bets. Equity solid (~45% vs her range), pot odds favorable for calls. Long-term, balances postflop defense. Check to realize equity and exploit her potential over-aggression.

Check. I opened to 300 and Elon flat‑called; the flop A♠ Q♥ T♦ is very connected and heavily favors his defending range (Ax, Qx, Tx, broadways). I have J♠5♦ — no pair, only four clean outs to a Broadway K and essentially no backdoor draw. Elon checked the flop and checked the turn (2♥), so betting the turn would be thin: many hands that beat me will call (or shove from a short stack) and I’d be risking chips with very little equity. With a big stack advantage I should exercise pot control, realize my tiny equity cheaply, and fold to significant aggression. Plan: check the turn (this action), call small bets to see a river if offered, fold to large shoves, and only consider a disciplined river bluff on a safe card if villain shows weakness.

River 7d is a low blank that doesn't significantly change the board dynamics (Td As Qh 2h 7d), adding no flush and minimal straight draws completing (e.g., 87 unlikely in range). With top pair Qc9h and weak 9-kicker OOP after check-check-check streets, Minni's button range is capped: unlikely Ax (would often c-bet flop), strong two-pair/sets (would bet for value), favoring missed draws, weak pairs (Jx-Tx-99-88), air from wide preflop open. Her aggressive history shows frequent preflop raises and flop bets, but rare double-checks suggest weakness here, exploiting by checking to induce folds earlier or showdown now. Value betting thin risks calls/raises from Ax/better Qx in her occasional trapping range and over-repping strength OOP; pot control preserves 3575 stack for better spots in 74/150 hand tournament. Check realizes equity (~60%+ vs her capped checking range), wins unimproved when ahead, loses minimal when behind. Balances prior checks, denies her free cards/steals, EV-maximal long-term vs exploitable button aggression.

Check. I have J♠5♦ with no pair or meaningful river improvement — only J-high. I opened as a button steal and exercised pot control by checking the very connected A‑Q‑T flop and the 2♥ turn. Elon flat‑called pre and then checked down, a line consistent with both slow‑played value (Ax, Qx, Tx, broadway pairs) and the occasional air, but against a generally passive caller my river bluff has weak fold equity and will be called by too many better hands. Pot is small relative to my stack and there’s no reason to bluff off chips with essentially no blockers; I’ll check to showdown and fold to any serious raise.

I am on the button with a pocket pair of deuces. In a three-handed game, any pocket pair is strong enough to open for a raise. I will raise to 300 to take the initiative and potentially win the blinds or play a pot in position. My goal is to either take it down pre-flop or flop a set for high value.

Preflop in SB with 4s6d offsuit, a weak gapped hand with no high card value or suitedness. Facing a button raise from Flash to 300 (pot 450, to call 250), which is ~20BB effective for me but poor odds. Postflop playability is terrible OOP against a wide button range. BB (Minni) has a massive stack and likely defends wide. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots. No history indicates Flash is super weak here to justify a call or 3bet.

Flash opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the big blind with 4♣7♠ (100 already posted). Calling another 200 into a 450 pot gives me ~30.8% break-even equity (200 / (450+200)). 4-7 offsuit has poor equity versus a button opening range, is frequently dominated, and is very hard to play out of position postflop. 3-betting as a bluff with this rag is also a bad frequency choice. With deep stacks and many better spots to pick, folding and preserving my stack is the +EV play here.

3-handed on button with 84o, a complete trash hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or high card value. Limp from SB Minni and check from BB Flash suggest wide ranges, but playing this hand risks chips with poor postflop playability and equity. Even in position, better to fold and wait for better spots to maximize stack long-term. No steal value here as hand is too weak.

I have 9♣6♣ in the small blind facing only the big blind. Pot is 150 and it costs 50 to complete. This is a playable suited connector with good postflop playability, but calling 50 leaves me in position/pot-control trouble and gives the BB easy odds to continue. I want to take the initiative and apply pressure to a single opponent with a wide defending range. Raising to 300 (3x the big blind total) is a standard steal size: large enough to fold out a lot of marginal hands and small enough to keep the pot manageable if called. If Flash folds I pick up the pot; if he calls I still have backdoor flush/straight equity and deep stacks for implied odds. If he 3-bets big I will usually fold and re-evaluate against smaller 3-bets. Overall raising maximizes my fold equity and postflop maneuverability in this spot.

I am in the Big Blind with 9 of hearts and 3 of hearts. Minni, the chip leader, has raised to 300 from the Small Blind. While I am getting 3:1 odds to call and the hand is suited, 9-3 is extremely weak and plays poorly out of position, especially against an aggressive big stack. I will fold to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

Button strategy: 8♠5♣ is a marginal hand, but on the button versus the blinds I want to be aggressive to pick up the dead money and play position. The pot is 150 and it's 100 to call — a standard 3x-ish open to 300 accomplishes three things: (1) follows the table's usual open sizing, (2) generates fold equity against the blinds, and (3) keeps the pot manageable if either opponent (especially Elon with ~40bb) decides to defend or 3-bet. Plan: if called, use positional advantage to c-bet favorable flops or check/control the runout; fold to a large 3-bet shove or highly polarizing 3-bet since 85o has poor equity against that range. Raise to 300 to steal and take initiative.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Deuce offsuit. In a three-handed game, this hand has some value against a Button open, but playing out of position against Minni, the chip leader, is difficult. Additionally, Elon in the Big Blind is relatively short-stacked and may look to shove over a call or a raise. Given the tournament goal of maximizing my stack over 150 hands and my current position as second in chips, I will choose a disciplined fold here to avoid a potentially marginal and expensive situation out of position.

Facing a standard button raise to 3x the BB from Minni, who has been aggressive with opens throughout the history. My A2o in BB is a clear defend: excellent pot odds (additional 200 into 450, ~2.25:1 immediate odds, pot becomes 650), Ace blocker reduces her AA/Ax frequency, decent equity (~35-45% vs wide BTN range including broadway, suited connectors, Ax). Post-flop OOP: check-fold floats/misses (frequent), extract value on Ace/flushdraw/straightdraw hits. Folding too tight (exploits her wide range); 3-bet/ shove overplays marginal Ax OOP with 40BB depth, invites calls from her massive stack. Balanced call mixes with monsters/speculative hands for max EV over 150 hands.

Flop: 5s 9s 3h gives me Ace-high with the nut flush draw (As), 9 clean outs to nuts (~35% equity standalone). Preflop: Defended wide A2o OOP profitably vs Minni's aggressive button open. Pot 650, acting first OOP vs deep-stacked aggressor (27k). Betting risks folding out her bluffs/floats while over-repping strength; check induces cbets from her wide range (Ax, broadway, pairs <9, missed draws), sets up check-raise shove (3675 into ~1000-1500 pot for 2.5-3:1), max value from worse (KQo+, 88, flush draws), folds better (sets, 2p) rarely. Balanced with previous Ax defends; check-call thin, check-fold never. Over 150 hands, exploits her aggression without overplaying.

I opened preflop and got called heads-up. On 5♠ 9♠ 3♥ I have a pair of fives (5♣) with an 8♠ kicker — a vulnerable, low pair with only a backdoor flush possibility. As the preflop aggressor I want to continue taking the initiative: a c-bet will extract value from worse pairs/weak made hands and charge spade/float combos while still keeping the pot manageable. I’ll size about 50% pot (325) — large enough to deny good odds to many draws and get called by worse, small enough given Elon’s ~3.9k stack. Plan: if he folds I take it down; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn (check-control blanks, continue when I improve, fold or proceed cautiously to large check-raises/polarized shoves).

Nut flush draw with Ace blocker on spade-heavy board (5s9s3h), excellent equity (~40-50%+ vs Minni's wide button cbet range: Ax suited, broadway, mid pairs, missed draws). Preflop defended profitably OOP. Her small 325 cbet (half pot) into 650 screams wide aggression/polarization. Check-raise all-in (3875) maximizes fold equity against bluffs/floats/worse (88-TT, flush draws, gutshots), extracts from pairs/top pair, rarely called by better (sets, 2-pair infrequent). Calling OOP risks turn cards completing her draws while facing bigger bets; smaller raise invites floats. Shoving ~12x her bet exploits her history of folding to aggression (e.g., Hands 1,9,14,22+), builds stack variance positively over 150 hands with 38BB effective.

I c‑bet 325 on the 5♠‑9♠‑3♥ flop as planned, but Elon shoved to 3,875 (I must call 3,550 into a 4,850 pot). That means I need ≈3550/(4850+3550) ≈ 42.3% equity to break even. My hand (8♠5♣) is just a vulnerable bottom pair with a marginal backdoor spade. Clean outs to improve to trips/two‑pair are only the two remaining 5s and three 8s (5 unique outs → ~20% to hit by river); making a backdoor flush requires both turn+river spades (~4% more). Even combining those, my chance to legitimately get ahead is roughly ~24–25% — well below the ~42% required. Elon’s all‑in after calling preflop is very likely to contain a high frequency of hands that already beat me (9x, sets, two‑pair, or spade+overcard combos) plus a few semi‑bluffs. With a big stack advantage and my prior plan to fold to a highly polarized shove, the right play is to preserve chips and fold rather than call a large, low‑equity all‑in.

I am on the button with T3o, which is a very weak hand. The pot is currently 150 (100 from Minni's big blind and 50 from Elon's small blind). Given the weakness of the hand and the fact that I am in the earliest position to act pre-flop among the active players (Button), there is no reason to enter the pot or raise. Folding is the standard and most profitable play here.

Button (Flash) has folded, leaving me in SB vs BB (Minni). Th9c is a playable hand with straight potential and decent high card value. With effective stacks (my 4800 ~48BB), this is a standard spot to min-raise for fold equity and to steal the dead money in the pot (150). Minni defends wide based on history, but raising small applies pressure and realizes equity postflop in position. Folding weak offsuit hands here would be too tight; completing limps too passively. Raising to 250 (2.5BB) is balanced and consistent with prior aggression.

I have a small pocket pair (3d3h) in the big blind facing a 250 open from the small blind. This is a classic set-mining spot — calling 150 more (total 250 committed) is a cheap way to see a flop and try to hit a set, while avoiding bloating the pot with a hand that performs poorly when it’s up against overcards or bigger pairs. A small 3-bet (to 400) is too weak to achieve fold equity and a big 3-bet/shove risks a costly flip vs higher pairs or a 4-bet shove from his ~46BB stack. I’ll call 250, see the flop, and only commit heavily if I hit a set or a very favorable texture; otherwise I’ll proceed cautiously vs strong action.

Preflop I raised with T9o from SB for value/thin value and fold equity against a limping BB defender. Flop 5c5h2s is a dry paired board that heavily favors my raising range: sets (55,52+), overpairs (77+), and some Ax. My hand is pure air with two overcards but no draws, but this is a perfect spot for a continuation bet as a bluff due to high fold equity. Minni's calling range OOP includes floats, weak Ax, suited broadway, and marginal pairs, many of which fold to c-bet on this board. OOP, betting denies equity and builds pot when ahead (though unlikely here). Size 60% pot (300 into 500) balances value/bluffs and leaves room for turns/rivers. Her history shows she calls stations but folds to repeated aggression (e.g., Hands 14,17,29). Checking risks giving free cards or facing bets with nothing.

I called preflop as a set-mining play with 3♦3♥ and missed — flop is 5♣5♥2♠. Elon (preflop raiser) fires a small bet (300 into an 800 pot). With bottom pair (threes) I’m behind any 5, any 2, and any higher pocket pair, but I beat a large portion of a typical c‑betting raiser’s missed range (broadways, Ax, etc.). Calling 300 into the 1100 effective pot after my call gives ~27% pot odds, which is reasonable to see a turn given my preflop plan to proceed cautiously unless I hit. Raising would bloat the pot with a weak hand and is a thin bluff against a preflop raiser; folding concedes good pot odds on a cheap turn card. I’ll call the 300 to reevaluate on the turn — if I improve (set) or the board gives me reason to barrel, I’ll commit; otherwise I’ll fold to sizable turn aggression.

Preflop raise with T9o from SB was a standard blind steal attempt against a limper/defending BB. Flop c-bet on the paired 5c5h2s board was a good bluff spot due to high fold equity against her wide OOP calling range (weak Ax, suited broadways, small pairs). She called, which assigns her some equity like 55/52 sets, 66-JJ, A5s/A2s, backdoors. Turn Kd is a relatively blank card for both ranges but improves some Kx, Ax hands in her range and adds possible flush draws if spades. Board remains dry, no flush or straight obvious. My hand has zero equity now: no pair, no draws (T-high gutshot unlikely), pure air. Continuing to bluff into a caller OOP risks her having showdown value or trapping sets. With 43BB stack and deep villain, checking is optimal pot control - induces bluffs from missed draws/weak pairs, allows cheap river if checked behind, and avoids bloating pot OOP with nothing. Her history shows calling tendencies but folding to post-oak aggression in spots; however, after calling flop, triple-barrel bluff is -EV here. Check to realize fold equity or showdown if weak.

I set‑mined preflop with 3♦3♥ and never improved — the board 5♣5♥2♠K♦ leaves me with a weak bottom pair. Elon (SB) checked the turn after c‑betting the flop, which can be either a missed overcard/air or a slowplayed K/5. I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal hand against a preflop raiser, and there’s good value in pot control heads‑up: checking lets me see a free river card and evaluate further. If Elon fires a small probe on the river I can call or fold depending on size; if he shows strength (big bet/shove) I should let this one go. With a deep stack advantage I can avoid unnecessary risk and keep options open — check and re‑evaluate on the river.

Preflop raise with T9o from SB was a standard blind defense/steal against BB limper. Flop c-bet on paired dry 552 board was optimal bluff with fold equity against her wide OOP calling range (weak Ax, suited connectors, small pairs). Her call assigns medium strength like 66-JJ, A5s/A2s, 52s, or stubborn floats. Turn Kd is neutral-ish (helps some Kx/Ax but bricks draws); checking pot control with air OOP after she called flop avoids bloating pot further (-EV triple barrel into caller). She checks behind, representing caution with non-nutted hands (full houses/sets less likely to check turn). River Jh is a blank that doesn't complete obvious draws (no flush, gutshots like AT/JT unlikely in her range) but pairs board slightly and adds straight possibilities (e.g., 34 for wheel unlikely). With pure air (T-high, no pair/draw), first to act in 1100 pot (39BB eff.), checking realizes equity: induces bluffs from missed draws/weak pairs or cheap showdown vs her likely 5x/SDV hands. Bluffing river over-reps nuts (55/52/K5/J5 full houses) but her history shows calling stations (e.g., Hands 2,14,51); post-check-check dynamics weaken rep, pot odds favor her hero-call with medium strength. Checking exploits her wide range while preserving stack for better spots in tournament.

I never hit my set — I have bottom pair (3s) on 5♣5♥2♠ K♦ J♥. Elon c‑bet small on the flop then checked both turn and river, which looks more like a missed c‑bet or medium-strength hand than a strong slowplay. Many holdings in his preflop-raising range (Kx, Jx, pocket pairs ≥4, any 5) beat my pair, so betting here would often get called by better hands and only fold pure air. With the pot only 1100 and no river bet to charge, the +EV play is pot control and take the free showdown. I will check and show down.

Preflop on the button with J6s suited connectors. Excellent position with only the big blind left to act, as Small Blind Minni likely folded (pot 150 suggests blinds posted and SB out). J6s plays well multiway or heads-up due to straight/flush potential and fold equity. Raising builds the pot, takes initiative, and frequently steals blinds given Flash's history of folding to raises (e.g., Hands 52, 54). Standard sizing around 3x BB. My stack of 4300 (43BB) allows flexibility. Raise to 300 for value and isolation.

I'm in the small blind with 3♣6♣ facing a button open to 300 (pot 450). To call is 250 more (300 total), min‑raise to 500. 3‑6 suited is very speculative and performs poorly out of position, especially multiway. If I call and the big blind folds I need ~36% equity (250/(450+250)); if the big blind also defends my required equity is still ~28% (250/900). Between being OOP, likely dominated by a button raiser’s wide but often stronger range, and the big blind’s propensity to defend, this hand has poor realized equity and little post‑flop playability. With a healthy stack I can wait for better spots; I don't want to spew chips with a marginal drawing hand OOP. Fold and preserve stack for a better position or stronger holding.

I am in the Big Blind with 8h 7s. Elon, the Button, has raised to 300. I have 200 more to call into a pot that will be 550 total. My stack is 7850, and Elon's stack is quite short at 4000. While 87o is a marginal hand, the price to see a flop is reasonable (2:1). However, against a Button raise from a shorter stack, Elon's range is likely stronger than my 87o. Looking at recent hands, Elon has been active but foldable. That said, out of position with a weak connector, I'd rather wait for a better spot to utilize my stack against the shorter-stacked Elon or the chip leader Minni. I will fold this hand and look for a more advantageous situation.

I’m on the button with Q♦5♥ — a weak but playable hand in 3‑handed play. With 50/100 blinds the pot is small and position is powerful, so I’ll open a standard button steal. A 3x open to 300 applies pressure to the blinds (Flash is loose/aggressive but often defends; Elon is a medium stack) while risking very little of my deep stack. Plan: fold to a sizable 3‑bet, call small defenses, c‑bet favourable flops (and proceed cautiously if called), and avoid bloating the pot without a made hand.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Ten offsuit, which is a very strong hand in a three-handed game. Minni, the chip leader, opened from the button. To take control of the pot and prevent the short-stacked Big Blind (Elon) from seeing a cheap flop or realizing equity, I will 3-bet. A raise to 1000 is a standard size that puts pressure on the button and defines the strength of my hand.

Preflop in BB with K4s, facing a button open to 300 and SB 3-bet to 1000. Pot 1400, to call 900 (pot odds ~39%). K4s is a speculative suited hand but weak kicker and poor playability OOP vs likely strong 3-bet range from Flash, who has shown aggression. Minni deep but likely strong. Equity likely <35% vs polarized range. Stacks allow folding marginal spots to wait for better. Preserve stack for 70 more hands.

Facing a fairly standard 3‑bet to 1000 after my 3x button open, Q♦5♥ is a weak, easily‑dominated hand with poor playability post‑flop. Calling 700 for ~25% pot odds still leaves me in tough spots against the SB’s 3‑bet range (pairs, broadways, strong Ax) and risks bloating the pot out of position or facing a 4‑bet. I prefer to preserve my deep stack and wait for a better spot — fold to this sizable 3‑bet.

I am on the button with Qs Jc, which is a strong opening hand in 3-handed play. My goal is to exert pressure from position and build a pot against the shorter stack (Elon) and the chip leader (Minni). A standard 3x open is appropriate here to take control of the hand.

Preflop in SB facing a Button raise to 300 (to call 250 into 450 pot). Holding 5s6d offsuit connectors, which have some playability but poor postflop playability OOP, especially multiway with BB to act. My stack is 4300 (~43BB), shortest at table. Flash likely wide from Button, but calling OOP with marginal hand risks difficult spots ahead, low implied odds due to stack sizes. Folding preserves stack for better positions or stronger hands. No strong reason to 3bet bluff or shove given hand strength and positions.

I’m in the big blind with A♣T♠ facing a button open to 300 (pot 450, I need 200). This is a strong, playable hand that blocks a lot of the button’s best A‑combos and plays well both as a 3‑bet and postflop. With healthy stacks (I have 27300, Flash 7950) I want to apply pressure and isolate the opener rather than passively calling and playing OOP. A standard 3‑bet to around 3x the open gives good fold equity and extracts value from worse A/x, Kx and broadway hands; a min‑3bet to 500 would be too small and invite too many cheap flops. I’ll 3‑bet to 1000 total. If he 4‑bets large/all‑in I’ll re‑evaluate (likely fold to an ICM‑unfavorable shove), if he calls I can c‑bet or continue on favorable textures and play for a bigger pot with position reads.

I opened QJo from the button, and Minni (the chip leader) 3-bet me from the Big Blind. While QJo isn't the strongest hand, I am in position and getting decent pot odds (700 to win a total pot of 2050). Minni has been active, and her 3-betting range from the blinds in a 3-handed game is likely quite wide. Calling allows me to evaluate the flop while maintaining a healthy stack relative to the short stack, Elon.

I hold A♣T♠ on Jh‑7c‑Th: top pair with the best kicker. I 3‑bet preflop and the button called, so his range contains Jx, Tx, Ax, broadways and heart draws. I'm out of position, so I want to both get value from worse tens/Kx/Ax and deny equity to the two‑heart draw. A ~55–60% pot c‑bet is the right balance here — large enough to charge draws and get value, small enough to keep fold equity and avoid bloating the pot given the effective stack (~7,250). I will bet 1,200 into the 2,050 pot. If he calls I will reevaluate on the turn and continue for value/protection; if he check‑raises very large (polarized) I will re‑assess and likely fold to an overcommitment.

On a flop of Jh-7c-Th, I have flopped top pair with a Queen kicker. While the board is quite coordinated (offering straight draws like 89, KQ, Q9 and flush draws), top pair in a three-handed game is a very strong holding. Minni 3-bet pre-flop and is now leading for about 60% of the pot. Her range includes overpairs (AA, KK, QQ), better Jacks (AJ, KJ), but also many draws and air that she would play aggressively as the chip leader. Folding here would be too tight. Raising likely isolates me against hands that have me beaten (sets, AJ, overpairs) while folding out her bluffs. By calling, I maintain pot control, utilize my position, and can re-evaluate on the turn based on the card and her subsequent action.

Check. I have A♣T♠ — a pair of tens (second pair) — and the K♥ on the turn makes the texture much more hostile: J♥‑7♣‑T♥‑K♥ is three hearts on board, so any two‑heart combo in Flash’s calling range just made a flush, and Kx/Jx/sets also beat me. Out of position with a medium‑strength hand and sizable effective stacks, my priority is pot control and information. Betting here invites a check‑raise/shove from hands that already crush me; checking lets me see what Flash does and keeps bluffs/weaker pairs in. Plan: check now; call a small‑to‑medium turn bet for pot control, but fold to a large shove or polarized check‑raise.

The Turn card (Kh) is an overcard to my Jack but also provides me with an open-ended straight draw (any Ace or 9). Minni, who 3-bet pre-flop and led the flop, has checked. This check could signal a loss of momentum, a desire for pot control with a hand like AJ or QQ, or a trap with a King. Given the wet board and the fact that I have significant equity with my pair and straight draw, checking back is the most prudent play. It allows me to realize my equity for free, prevents me from being check-raised off my draw by the chip leader, and keeps the pot manageable while I still hold some showdown value with second pair.

Board: Jh‑7c‑Th‑Kh‑2h. I hold A♣T♠ — second pair with the best kicker, but the board is now very scary: four hearts by the river means any single heart in Flash’s hand is a made flush. He called my flop c‑bet and then checked turn and river, which looks like either a weak showdown hand or a missed draw rather than an aggressive value hand. Betting here would risk being called (or raised) by many hands that beat me (any heart, Kx, some Jx or sets) while only folding out the hands I beat — thin value at best and big downside when wrong. With healthy stack depth and the ability to realize showdown value, pot control is the prudent line. I will check and take this to showdown rather than build the pot and run into a flush or better hand.

On the river, the board now shows four hearts (Jh, Th, Kh, 2h), which makes any single heart in an opponent's hand a flush. I hold Qs Jc, which is a pair of Jacks with no heart. Minni 3-bet pre-flop and led the flop but has checked both the turn and the river. While her checks might indicate a weak hand or a missed draw, the board is extremely coordinated with both straight and flush possibilities. I have modest showdown value with my pair of Jacks, but I am unlikely to get called by a worse hand if I bet, and I certainly won't fold out a King or a flush. Checking back is the only sensible play to see the showdown and minimize further loss on a dangerous board.

Preflop on the button with K♦3♦, a suited king with good playability. Assuming action has folded to me (no prior actions listed), this is a standard spot to raise for value, isolation against blinds, or blind steal. Position is excellent for postflop play. Minni has a massive stack but we have fold equity, especially vs Flash in BB. Raising to 3x the BB (300 total) is standard sizing. Folding or limping is suboptimal with this hand in position.

I’m in the small blind with 4♦3♣ facing Elon’s raise to 300 (pot 450) and a big blind behind with a healthy stack. Calling 250 more (total commit 300) would require ~35.7% equity to be break-even (250/700). 4‑3 offsuit is very weak: heavily dominated by a button opening range, poor playability out of position, and its equity collapses further if the big blind defends. A raise would be a pure bluff with poor blockers and risks getting isolated or 3-bet by deeper stacks. I’ll conserve chips and wait for a better spot — fold.

I have Ace-Five of hearts in the big blind, which is a very strong semi-premium hand in a three-handed game. Elon, on the button, has raised to 300, and my stack significantly covers his remaining 3700 chips. While I could flat call, a 3-bet is effective here to put pressure on a wide button opening range and potentially take the pot down pre-flop. Given the stack sizes, raising to 900-1000 is a standard play to exert leverage.

Facing a sizable 3bet (to 1000) from Flash in the BB after my button open to 300. K♦3♦ is a suited king but the 3 makes it weak and easily dominated by better kings in Flash's likely strong 3bet range (premiums like QQ+, AK, maybe some bluffs but BB 3betting wide enough doesn't change much). Position postflop is good (act last), but equity realization is poor without hitting the nut flush draw or top king with good kicker. Pot odds: 700 to call into ~2050 pot requires ~25% equity, but vs a typical BB 3bet range (15-20% hands), K3s has ~30-35% equity raw but lower realized due to reverse dominance and playability issues. Raising/shoving 4000 risks 4BB stack to win ~13BB pot, fold equity uncertain vs aggro Flash but ICM/tournament pressure with 40BB stack favors waiting for better. Folding preserves stack for superior spots in this multiway aggressive game.

Q6o is a marginal hand but on the button it's cheap to see a flop for 100 into a 150 pot and I have position on both opponents. Flash (SB) has shown willingness to 3-bet and Elon (BB) is ~39bb and could shove or overcommit if I open-raise — I don't want to get isolated or stacked with a weak hand. Calling keeps the pot small, preserves postflop flexibility, and lets me play position — I will proceed cautiously postflop, looking to continue only with a real piece (pair or good draw) and folding to heavy resistance on dry boards.

In the Small Blind with Jack-Six offsuit, I am facing a limp from the Button (Minni). While the price to complete is very attractive (50 chips to win a 250 pot, giving 5:1 odds), J6o is a very weak hand that plays poorly out of position post-flop. It is easily dominated by Minni's limping range and Elon's potential Big Blind range. Folding here preserves my stack and avoids difficult marginal situations in a multi-way pot where I'll be first to act on every street.

JT suited is a strong speculative hand with good playability, straight and flush potential. Facing a button limp from Minni, who often plays wide ranges based on hand history (frequent calls, some limps with speculative hands). As BB OOP with 39BB stack, raising isolates the limper, builds the pot when ahead in equity, and denies equity to weaker hands. Min raise is 200 total, but sizing to 400 (4x the limp) is standard for isolation, providing fold equity while keeping SPR reasonable postflop. Minni's deep stack (250BB+) means she can defend wide, but our hand realizes well multiway or heads-up. Checking risks multiway pots OOP with worse hands dominating.

I limp-called earlier with Q6o to see a cheap flop in position. Elon’s raise to 400 puts 300 more to call into a ~550 pot; Q6o is too weak to 3-bet and I don’t want to isolate against a shorter-stacked opponent who could shove or overcommit. Calling keeps the pot controlled, preserves my positional advantage, and lets me fold to heavy aggression on a dry flop or continue when I pair the queen/six or hit a draw. I’ll call 300 more (400 total) and proceed cautiously postflop.

Flop Ks6c7c hits my JcTc with a strong club flush draw (9 outs) and two live overcards (T,J blockers), giving excellent equity (~35-45% vs typical calling ranges). Board is drawy with flush and straight draws (45,89,58), favoring my range as preflop raiser/isolator. OOP heads-up vs Minni's loose calling range from limp-call (per history: frequent calls with speculative/wide hands). C-bet semi-bluffs for thin value from Ax,Kx weak pairs, charges her draws, builds pot with equity advantage, denies equity/realization. 2/3 pot sizing (600 into 850) balances value/protection/fold equity without overbetting SPR (~5:1 effective). Check risks giving free card to her equity or facing raise; betting maintains initiative maximizes EV long-term.

Call 600. I have middle pair (6s) with a Queen kicker on Ks-6c-7c and I’m in position. Elon is short (≈3000) and opened wide preflop, and this 600 into a 1450 pot is a relatively small c-bet that looks like a continuation bet or a probe for folds rather than a massive value shove. Calling keeps the pot controlled, lets me use position to get more information on the turn, and realizes equity against his wide range (there are clubs and straight draws to worry about). A raise would risk getting isolated or shoved by a stronger K/7/set and needlessly bloat the pot with a marginal made hand. Plan: call, re-evaluate on the turn — bet or raise only if I improve or the turn is favorable; fold to large, committed aggression if I don’t improve.

Turn 10h pairs my Tc for top pair with Jack kicker, while preserving the nut club flush draw (9 outs). Board Ks6c7c10h is coordinated with flush draws, OESD (89), and possible two-pair/set mining. Heads-up OOP vs Minni's button limp-call range (wide per history: speculative suited connectors, Ax suited clubs, mid-pairs, suited broadways). She called flop c-bet into drawy board, representing clubs/draws/weak Kx/76/67. My hand has ~60-70% equity vs her range (beats weak pairs/one-pair, draws ahead, behind sets/TT/JJ/sets only). Pot 2050, stack 3000 (SPR ~1.5:1), bet for value/protection: charges remaining draws (spade flush unlikely), gets value from Kx/76/weak pairs, denies equity to floats/Ax clubs. Size to 1400 (~2/3 pot) builds pot for river jam (leaves ~1600), balances value/fold equity without bloating pot underequity. Check risks free river (brings flush/completes draws), facing her bet/raise. Betting maximizes EV exploiting her loose history (frequent calls deep-stacked).

Call 1400. I have middle pair (6s) with a decent queen kicker on a K-6-7-T board and I’m in position. Elon is very short and his turn raise is effectively a near-all-in — but the pot (3450) gives me ~29% equity threshold (call 1400 to win ~4850). Against a short-stack shove his range will include lots of value (Kx, Tx, sets, straights) but also many bluffs/draws and broadways he opened with preflop. My hand has reasonable equity vs that mixed range, and raising myself would be spewy (I’d either isolate or get shoved on). Folding concedes a large pot with good pot odds to continue. I’ll call the 1400, see the river, and then re-evaluate — fold to any further clear value shove if I get proper information that I’m behind.

River 3c completes my club flush draw, giving me the J-high flush (Jc Tc with 7c 6c 3c). This is a very strong hand on a coordinated board with flush draws completed. No obvious straights (possible 89 straight from turn, but unchanged). Heads-up OOP vs Minni's wide limp-call range (suited connectors, broadways, Ax suited, pairs per history of frequent calls deep-stacked). She called flop and turn cbets into draw-heavy board, representing clubs/draws/weak pairs/Kx. My hand crushes her one-pair, lower flushes (e.g., 954c, suited junk clubs), two-pair; loses to higher flushes (AcXc, KcXc, QcXc), sets (KK/66/77 unlikely). Pot 4850 > my 1600 stack (SPR <0.4), so jamming extracts max value from her loose calling station tendencies (history: calls big bets with marginal holdings). Checking risks free showdown (she checks worse/scared) or facing bet where I have to call off anyway with strong hand. Shoving denies equity, balances with missed draws bluffs in range, exploits her deep stack/passivity. EV-maximizing play to grow stack in tournament.

Heads-up vs Elon who has just put his last 1,600 in and is effectively all‑in. Current pot = 6,450; calling 1,600 would make the total pot 8,050, so I need ~1,600/8,050 ≈ 19.9% equity to break even. My hand: Qh6d — middle pair (6s) with a decent queen kicker, no clubs. Board: Ks 6c 7c Th 3c. The river 3c completed possible club flushes, and Elon's line (raise pre, bet/raise flop, big turn bet, shove river) contains many value hands (Kx, T×, 8‑9 straights, sets, and now club flushes) but also, given his short stack and wide opening, a nontrivial number of bluffs/semi‑bluffs/missed broadways. Against a realistic mixed all‑in range my pair of sixes still has roughly ~20–30% equity (it fares well vs A‑high and lower pairs and some missed broadways), which is at or above the required ~20% threshold. Raising would be meaningless (he's all‑in) and folding here would be folding correct pot odds away for a small cost relative to my stack. Conclusion: call the 1,600 to see showdown.

I am on the button with Queen-Jack offsuit, which is a very strong hand in a three-handed game. Raising here allows me to take control of the pot and use my positional advantage throughout the hand. A standard 3x raise is appropriate.

J♦4♣ is a very weak offsuit hand with no connectivity, high card value, or suited potential. Facing a button raise from Flash, who has been aggressive in prior hands, in the small blind position. Defending this hand is unprofitable as it has poor playability postflop and low equity against a wide button range. Pot odds are not sufficient (calling 250 into 450 pot ~36% pot, but equity is much lower). Best to fold and preserve stack for better spots.

Flash opened to 300 from the button and Elon folded, so it’s heads-up. My hand 9h3h is weak but suited — not a 3-bet candidate out of position. Pot is 450 and I must add 200 more (total 300) to call; that gives ~31% pot odds. Flash’s button open will be fairly wide, and with deep stacks I have reasonable implied odds to justify a defend with a suited hand that can hit flushes or two-pair/straights occasionally. Plan: call to see a flop, fold to largecontinued aggression on dry boards, and play aggressively only if I pick up a pair or strong draw. I’ll flat-call rather than raise to preserve stack and keep weaker parts of his range in.

Out of position with 9♥3♥ on a monotone Q♣5♣2♣ flop. I have no pair, no club draw, and this board heavily favors the preflop raiser’s range. With a healthy stack and a heads‑up open, there’s no reason to barrell or bluff into that range — I should control the pot and preserve chips. I’ll check here and fold to meaningful continuation bets. If Flash checks back I’ll see a free turn and only continue when I pair or pick up a strong draw; I might call a very small probe but will fold to sizeable C‑bets (especially ≳1/3–1/2 pot).

I have flopped top pair with a Jack kicker on a monotone board. Holding the Jack of clubs is significant as it provides both a blocker to some of the opponent's potential flushes and gives me a redraw to a higher flush if a fourth club appears. Minni has checked to me, the pre-flop aggressor. I should place a continuation bet here to extract value from weaker queens, smaller pairs, or hands containing a single high club. A bet of roughly 60% of the pot is appropriate to charge any draws while not overcommitting if Minni is already holding a made flush.

Out of position with 9♥3♥ on a monotone Q♣5♣2♣ flop. I checked and Flash fired 400 into a 1050 pot — I must call 400 to win 1450, which requires about 27.6% equity. I have no pair, no club draw, only backdoor hearts and no meaningful blockers to his range. The BTN’s open/continuation range includes many clubs and queens, so his bet heavily disfavors my holding. Raising would be a pure bluff without good blockers and would risk too much of my stack. Given my very poor equity and the sizable bet, folding is the correct, chip‑preserving play.

Preflop on the button with 5s8c, offsuit gappers with low cards and poor playability. Weak hand that doesn't warrant entering the pot voluntarily. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots. Position is good but hand too weak for limp/call/raise.

Heads-up from the small blind with 9♠7♣ — a marginal but playable hand. The pot is 150 and I only need to put in 50 more; calling 50 (100 total committed including my posted SB) gives me ~25% pot odds to see the flop and I keep the postflop positional advantage (I act last after the flop). A min-raise to 200 is unlikely to fold out a wide BB defending range and would just bloat the pot with a marginal hand. Plan: call cheaply, evaluate the flop and continue only with a pair, good draw, or favorable texture; otherwise concede to aggression.

I am in the Big Blind with 9-6 offsuit. The Button folded and the Small Blind (Minni) completed. I have the option to check or raise. Given that I have position on Minni for the rest of the hand and 9-6 offsuit is a marginal hand, checking to see a free flop is the most consistent and low-risk strategy. Raising here would turn a mediocre hand into a bluff unnecessarily.

I have 9♠7♣ on A♠–4♠–4♣ — I completely missed. The flop heavily favors the BB’s defending range: lots of Ax combos and pocket pairs (including 4x) that beat me. I have essentially no showdown value and only a very slim runner‑runner/spade plan, so bluffing here is low EV — many hands will simply call a c‑bet and I’ll be behind. With deep stacks and position I’ll keep the pot small and see what Flash does; checking preserves options, lets me fold to strong pressure, and allows me to call a small probe to realize equity and potentially bluff later on appropriate cards. Plan: check and fold to large value bets; call small probing bets because of position and the chance to take the pot away on later streets if villain shows weakness.

I have 9-6 offsuit and the flop is 4s-As-4c. I have no pair and no draw. Minni checked to me in the Big Blind. There is very little reason to bluff this board as it favors an Ace, which is well within a Small Blind's completing range. I will check behind and hope to improve on the turn or simply reach a cheap showdown.

I have 9♠7♣ on A♠–4♠–4♣–T♠. I completely missed — no pair and only a one-card flush draw (9 spade outs, ~19% to hit on the river). Both players checked the flop which signals a lot of medium/weak hands and slow‑played A/4x; betting here into that range will often get called and bloat a pot I’m not happy committing to. With deep stacks and a small pot (200) the low‑variance, higher EV line is to check and keep the pot controllable, realizing my fold equity only vs very weak holdings. I’ll call small probing bets on the turn/river to try to hit or to capitalize on cheap odds, but will fold to large turn aggression since I have essentially no showdown value.

I have 9-6 offsuit with no pair and no real draw on a board of 4s-As-4c-Ts. The pot is very small (200), and Minni has checked to me twice. There is no reason to bluff here as I have zero equity and I don't want to inflate the pot with a weak hand. I will check behind to see a free river card and hope to either hit something or simply take it to a cheap showdown.

The river 8♠ completed a backdoor spade flush for me (As–Ts–8s–4s with my 9♠ = Ace‑high spade flush). This is a valueable, made hand but not the nut — any K♠/Q♠/J♠ in Flash’s range now beat me. However both players were passive (checked flop and turn), which weights villain’s range toward Ax, pocket pairs and missed draws rather than a two‑spade combo that already had a flush on the turn. With the pot only 200, a small value bet will extract calls from worse hands (A‑x, medium pairs, missed broadway hands) while keeping the pot manageable if I’m up against a higher spade. I’ll bet a half‑pot probe of 100 to get value and charge non‑spade showdown hands. If Flash raises large I’ll re‑evaluate (large raises are usually indicative of a better spade and I won’t overcommit).

On a board of 4s-As-4c-Ts-8s, I have 9-high with no pair or flush. Minni has bet 100 into a 200 pot after checking all previous streets. While the bet is small, I have zero showdown value and cannot even beat a bluff from a hand like King-high. There is no reason to risk chips or attempt a bluff for such a small pot when my hand has no equity.

7♣3♦ is one of the weakest hands, but I’m on the button in a 3‑handed pot so my open‑range should be very wide. Folding surrenders too much dead money; calling 100 (limping) invites multiway pots where this hand performs terribly. A small, standard steal — min‑raise to 2x BB (200 total) — applies pressure to the blinds and preserves positional advantage. Plan: fold to a cold 3‑bet with this junk hand, but if called I’ll proceed very cautiously postflop (check most missed flops, use position to bluff selectively or fold to substantial resistance). Raise to 200.

I am in the small blind with 2c 9s, which is an extremely weak hand. Minni has raised from the button to 200. Although the price to call is only 150 more, playing 92o out of position against a deep stack like Minni is a losing proposition in the long run. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button raise to 200 (100 to call into 350 pot). My hand J5o is weak, offsuit, poor connectivity, and low playability postflop. Minni's button range is wide but still favors folding trash like this to avoid difficult spots OOP with minimal equity.
