Pot odds are decent (~3.5:1) but not enough given the hand's poor implied odds and reverse implied odds potential. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

I am on the button with 7-5 offsuit. While it's a weak hand, I'm at a 3-handed table where aggression from the button is generally rewarded. However, given the current stack sizes and the tendency of Minni (Big Blind) to be active, and the fact that 7-5 offsuit is a very weak starting hand with little post-flop playability, folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my 11,000 stack for better opportunities.

Preflop in SB with As2s after Button folds. This is a premium suited ace hand for stealing blinds from SB vs BB with deep stacks. As2s has excellent playability: high card strength, suited for flush potential, live ace blocker. Minni in BB has shown wide calling ranges and aggression, but raising takes initiative, builds pot when ahead, and applies pressure. Standard 3x BB raise to 300 total. Folding or limping too passive in position.

Elon opened to 300 from the small blind and I'm in the big blind with T4 offsuit. This is a weak hand, but the additional cost to defend is only 200 (total 300) and Elon raises a lot from the SB, so his range will be wide. I won't 3-bet with such a dominated holding — calling to see a flop is the lowest-variance, +EV line here: I can fold to strong aggression if I miss, and continue only with top-pair, good draws or a controlled plan when I improve.

Flop is Kd Qh 5h, a coordinated king-high board with heart flush draw. Holding As2s OOP for ace-high with backdoor spade flush draw and minimal straight potential. No pair, low showdown value against Minni's wide BB calling range (kings, broadways, suited connectors, pairs that connect well). Preflop aggression gives fold equity via c-bet, but deep stacks (7600 effective vs her 20800) and her history of calling stations (e.g., Hand 2 river call, Hand 14 multi-street calls) suggest checking for pot control. Betting air risks calls/raises from top pair/draws, turning hand face-up; checking allows free cards, induces her bluffs/bets I can fold to profitably, or cheap showdown if she checks weak hands. Standard pro play: check-fold marginal air OOP on wet board to preserve stack for better spots.

Board Qh-5h-Kd; I hold Ts-4d — complete air. Elon opened from SB wide and then checked the flop, which can be either pot-control with K/Qx or a delayed C-bet with lots of weak broadways/draws. I have no hearts and only ~6 clean outs (three tens, three fours) to make a pair, so my equity is low. With stacks deep and only one opponent, a free card and a cheap turn decision is the low-variance, +EV line: I’ll check and see a turn. If Elon bets small I can call for odds with my 6 outs; I’ll fold to significant pressure. No thin bluff or bluff-catch here — preserve chips and re-evaluate on the turn.

Turn pairs my deuce for bottom pair with ace kicker, improving from ace-high air. Board remains draw-heavy (hearts, emerging diamond flush draw, possible straight draws like JT or 34). Minni called preflop OOP and checked flop, representing a wide range including overpairs, top pair, mid pairs, draws, or floats. OOP, betting for thin value against worse pairs/draws (e.g., 5x, weaker K, pure draws) and protection against live draws. Checking risks giving free card to her draws or induces her bets I can fold cheaply, but pot control isn't urgent with ~12bb effective relative to pot. Half-pot bet sizes well for value/protection, maintains fold equity against bluffs, and builds pot when ahead. History shows Minni calls multi-street but folds to aggression when behind (e.g., Hand 14 river fold). Standard play: bet turn after check-check flop when improving.

I have T♠4♦ on Q♥5♥K♦2♦ — essentially air with only 6 clean river outs (three tens + three fours), ~6/46 ≈ 13% equity to improve on the river. Pot math: pot is 1,400 after Elon’s 400 turn bet and my cost to call is 400, so calling gives me ~400/(1,800) ≈ 22.2% required equity — calling is -EV (roughly -165 chips). Raising to the min (to 800) creates fold equity: if he folds I win the 1,400 pot immediately; if he calls the expected value at showdown (with my ~13% equity) is negative (~-113 chips). Breaking it down, a raise to 800 is +EV as long as he folds more than ≈7.5% of the time. Elon has been opening wide pre‑flop and betting small here (check on flop → small turn bet), so his range is often weak/made‑thin or bluffy and he will fold to aggression often enough. Plan: make a small, aggressive raise to take the pot now; if he 3‑bets large I will fold (he'll usually have a real hand), if he calls I'll proceed cautiously on the river (check or fold to sizable bets).

Facing a raise to 800 (400 to call) into an 1800 pot on the turn with bottom pair and ace kicker (2s paired the 2d) on a K♦ Q♥ 5♥ 2♦ board. Preflop: Raised A♠2♠ from SB, strong suited ace for initiative. Flop: Checked air OOP on wet king-high flush draw board for pot control. Turn: Bet thin value/protection after check-check when pairing deuce, as improved hand has showdown value against draws/bluffs and blocks strong aces/kings. Minni's raise polarizes to strong value (top pair Kx+, overpairs QQ+, sets) or semi-bluffs (heart flush draws like A♥X♥/J♥T♥, diamond draws, combos). Her BB calling range is wide, but checking flop then raising turn after my value-bet line suggests strength or nutted draw; history shows aggression but multi-street calls with medium strength (Hand 14, 51), yet folds rivers to pressure when behind. OOP, calling 400 (18% pot odds) risks spew: vulnerable to overcards, flushes completing (♥♦ possible), board texture favors her range. Effective stacks deep (7200 vs 20000), but no strong implied odds for suckouts (ace or spade unlikely to win). Raising/jamming overcommitment with marginal hand, poor vs value. Standard pro play: fold bottom pair facing turn raise OOP on draw-heavy board after thin value line gets countered; preserve stack for superior spots in high roller.

Q9o is a strong playable hand from the button position, especially facing a limp from SB and presumed check from BB. Position allows us to realize equity postflop effectively. Pot is 150, calling 100 offers good implied odds, but raising isolates the limper, takes initiative, and applies pressure on blinds with our stack sizes (72BB effective). Standard raise sizing to 4x the BB (400 total) to build pot while maintaining fold equity. Opponent tendencies from history show Minni calls wide preflop but folds to aggression often postflop; Flash similar. This maximizes EV by denying equity to worse hands and punishing limps.

Fold. I’m in the small blind with J♦2♥ (very weak offsuit) facing a button open to 400 into 50/100 blinds. The pot is 550 and it costs me 350 to call, so I’d need roughly 39% equity just to break even preflop (350/(550+350)). J2o is frequently dominated by a button opening range, I’ll be out of position postflop, and the big blind can still act or squeeze. A min-raise to 700 is an option but would be a thin bluff that invites 3-bets and multiway action — not worth it with this holding. I’ll preserve my healthy stack and fold.

I am in the Big Blind with T9s, a strong speculative hand. Elon opened for 4x from the Button, which is a bit large, but my hand has excellent playability and the pot odds are reasonable. Calling is standard here to see a flop with a hand that can flop well in terms of both equity and draw potential. Folding is too tight, and raising is an option but calling keeps the pot manageable and allows me to realize equity more easily given my stack size and position.

I am out of position against the pre-flop aggressor on a board that significantly favors a button opening range (which includes many Kings). I have no direct pair or draw, only marginal backdoor potential with ten-high. Checking is the standard and most prudent play to see how the opponent acts on this texture.

Preflop: Raised Q9o from button as a strong playable hand exploiting wide BB calling range, building pot with initiative. Flop: Kc8h3h is a good spot for continuation bet. No made hand but queen-high has showdown value vs missed draws/pairs; Qc blocks KQ, 9h gives backdoor flush/straight potential. BB called raise OOP so range includes Ax suited, suited connectors, mid pairs, broadway - many miss this king-high board with flush draw. Flash's check indicates weakness (no strong king/trap likely rare). C-bet ~60% pot (500 into 850) applies pressure, high fold equity, denies equity to floats/draws. Deep stacks (6800 eff) allow maneuverability postflop if check-raised. Aggression maximizes EV long-term in tournament.

I have Ten-high with no draws on a King-high board that favors the pre-flop aggressor's range. Elon's continuation bet of 500 into 850 is a strong sizing on this texture. Given I have zero equity and no significant backdoor potential, folding is the most disciplined and profitable play to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

I’m on the button with A♥4♦, pot 150, to call 100 (SB 50 / BB 100). In a three-handed game this is a clear open-raise spot: the hand has position, blocks many Ace combinations, and has decent fold equity. Table standard open size has been ~300 frequently, so I’ll make a standard 3x BB open rather than the minimum (200) to get meaningful fold equity and avoid giving cheap flops. Plan: if I take it down pre-flop great; if called I’ll play post-flop in position — c-bet for value on Ace‑high or dry boards, pot‑control/check vs coordinated/wet boards, and be willing to fold to heavy aggression on scary runouts. If I face a large 3‑bet (well above ~3x my raise) I will usually fold A4o; I may call a small, in‑position 3‑bet and proceed cautiously. Action: raise to 300.

I am holding Queen-Deuce offsuit (Q2o) in the Small Blind. This is a very weak hand with poor playability, especially when out of position against the button. Minni, the chip leader, has opened for 300 from the button. There is no strategic reason to defend this hand or attempt a bluff at this stage. Folding is the most disciplined and standard play to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

Preflop in the big blind with 6s9d offsuit, facing a button raise to 300 (200 to call into 450 pot). This hand is weak, gapped, offsuit, and plays poorly out of position postflop. Minni's button range is wide, but 6-9o lacks playability, strong equity realization OOP, or good blocker value for 3-betting as a bluff. Pot odds are decent (~2.25:1), but folding avoids difficult postflop spots and preserves stack for better opportunities in a long tournament (hand 89/150). History shows Minni aggressive but capable; no need to defend this trash.

I am on the button with 6s 5s, which are strong suited connectors in a three-handed game. Opening here is standard to put pressure on the blinds and capitalize on my positional advantage. A 3x raise is consistent with the established table dynamics and allows me to take control of the hand.

Preflop from Small Blind facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB). My hand is 4c2c, a very weak suited connector with poor playability, low equity against a typical button opening range. Out of position postflop, and with deep stacks (my 7500 effective), there's no reason to defend this trash hand. Folding preserves chips for better spots. History shows Flash is aggressive but this doesn't change the fundamentals.

I’m in the big blind with A♦T♥ facing a button open to 300 (pot 450, I need to add 200). Calling is the best default: pot odds = 200/(450+200) ≈ 30.8%, and A‑T offsuit has good equity versus a wide button opening range and carries an Ace blocker (reduces opponents’ AA/AK combos). A 3‑bet to ~900 could pick up the pot, but ATo is somewhat marginal to inflate a deep, out‑of‑position pot and Flash has shown willingness to continue/play back in many spots. I’ll defend by calling to see a flop and play post‑flop (look to continue on A‑high/transparent boards, fold to avoidable big 4‑bets, and realize equity on safer textures).

I check. On Js-6d-Jc I only have Ace-high (AhTd) and this paired board heavily favors the preflop raiser’s range (many Jx and pocket pairs). Leading out with air is unlikely to fold out many worse hands and will get called by a lot of hands I’m behind to. Checking controls pot size (pot = 650) and lets Flash show aggression so I can react — I’ll call reasonable c-bets to realize equity and float to the turn, fold to large check‑raises/overbets, and if he checks back I get a free card and can improve or seize initiative on a favourable turn.

I am the pre-flop aggressor on the button and have flopped middle pair (sixes) on a paired board (Jacks). While the pair of Jacks on the board makes the situation slightly delicate, my hand has significant showdown value. I will place a standard continuation bet of approximately 40% of the pot. This bet serves to protect my hand against overcards like KQ or AT, which have equity against me, and allows me to maintain the initiative. If called, I will likely look for a cheap showdown unless the board improves my hand specifically.

Call 250. The pot is 900 and Flash’s small flop bet of 250 gives me about 250/(900+250) ≈ 21.7% pot odds. A♥T♦ has roughly 6 clean outs to pair (A or T) and about ~27–30% chance to hit by the river, plus some additional showdown equity vs a wide c‑bet. Flash’s small sizing looks like a standard wide c‑bet on a paired Jxx board — there will be a lot of bluffs/missed overcards in his range and I also block some AJ combos. Raising as a bluff here is too risky with only Ace‑high (I can get re‑raised or put in awkward spots), and folding surrenders +EV prospects. I’ll call to realize equity, keep the pot controlled with deep stacks, and re‑evaluate on the turn — fold to heavy turn aggression if I miss, or raise/value on a favourable Ace/Ten turn.

I check. I have A♥T♦ on Js-6d-Jc-Qc and currently only Ace-high. There are only 6 clean outs (three Aces, three Tens) to pair on the river — ~13% to improve — so my raw equity to improve is low. Flash was the preflop raiser and made a small flop c-bet that I called; that line is consistent with a wide but often pair-y continuing range (Jx, sets, some pocket pairs and broadway/Qx combos). The Q on the turn actually improves a lot of his continuing hands to a clear value category (Qx, KQ, AQ, etc.).

Betting here as a bluff has limited fold equity: he already called the flop, so many Jx/Qx/pocket-pair hands will simply call a small turn bet, and bloat the pot out of position when I have no value. With deep stacks I don't want to manufacture a big pot with air. Checking controls the size, keeps my options, and allows me to fold to genuine strength. Plan: check and see what Flash does — call small-to-medium bets to realize my ~13% river outs and because of pot odds (I can call light when sizing is small), fold to large turn aggression/overbets, and if I hit an A or T on the river I can extract value; if I miss and he checks back I get a free showdown card, and if he bets the river I will fold unless the price is very good or I actually pair up.

I have a pair of sixes on a board that is paired with Jacks and now contains an overcard Queen. While I have showdown value, the Queen is a card that likely hits Minni's calling range from the Big Blind. By checking back, I control the size of the pot and avoid being check-raised off a hand that still has equity. This allows me to see the river for free and potentially reach a cheap showdown against missed draws or lower pocket pairs.

I have two pair (Jacks and Tens) with an Ace kicker on Js‑6d‑Jc‑Qc‑Tc — a very strong showdown hand but vulnerable to Jx, Qx and sets/full houses. Flash’s line (button open, small flop c‑bet, check on the turn and check on the river) looks like a wide/medium range that contains many missed overcards, medium pairs and showdown hands (AK/AQ/Kx/99–TT, some bluffs), and is not a clear value‑heavy line for trips/full houses. I checked earlier streets exactly to preserve the river for this exact scenario: now that I’ve paired my Ten, I should extract thin value. I’ll make a modest value bet (~45% pot) to get called by worse hands (Ax, Kx, weaker pairs, missed draws) while keeping the size small enough that a raise would be pretty polarized to hands that beat me. If Flash raises significantly I’ll treat it as a polarized/strong line and fold; if he calls I expect to be ahead often.

On the river, the board is J-6-J-Q-T with three clubs. I hold a pair of sixes, which was middle pair on the flop but has since been overtaken by the Queen and the Ten. Minni checked the flop and called my continuation bet, then checked the turn after the Queen appeared. Her lead of 500 on the river (into a 1150 pot) suggests she has either improved to a better pair (Qx or Tx), holds a Jack for trips, or completed a straight or flush. A pair of sixes is now effectively the bottom of the range and only beats a total bluff. Given the board texture and Minni's betting line, I don't have enough equity to justify a call.

Preflop on the button with A♥T♥, a strong suited ace hand with great playability and position. The pot is 150 after SB limp (to call 100) and BB check, indicating weakness. Standard play is to raise for value, to isolate the limper, build the pot, and take control. Raising to 300 (3x the BB) is a good size given the deep stacks and history of opponents calling light. This hand performs well multiway or heads-up postflop.

I’m in the small blind with J♠4♠ facing a button open to 300. J4s is suited and has some backdoor/playability, but it’s a weak hand and I’ll be out of position vs the button (and possibly the big blind). Folding is too tight given the cheap price to see a flop; a tiny 3‑bet to 500 would bloat the pot and get me into tricky spots with a marginal holding. Calling 250 (300 total) gives good pot odds to realize equity and keeps the pot manageable. Plan: see the flop, continue with pairs/strong draws or a decent jack/flush draw, and fold to heavy aggression when missed.

I am in the big blind with Jc 9h. Elon raised to 300 from the button, and Minni called from the small blind. I only need to call 200 more into a pot of 700, giving me 3.5-to-1 odds. J9o is strong enough to defend the big blind in a multi-way pot with these favorable odds.

Check. I called preflop to see this flop and I’ve missed: A♣ 7♥ 5♠ with J♠4♠. I only have a backdoor spade possibility (need both turn+river ≈ 4%) and no pair, while two opponents and an Ace on board make Ax and better hands common. Out of position, priority is pot control and folding to heavy aggression. I’ll check to see the turn and only continue if I pair (J or 4), pick up a real draw (a spade on the turn giving a 4‑card flush draw), or face a very small probe bet that offers correct pot odds; otherwise I’ll fold to significant bets.

I have Jack-high on an Ace-high board in a multi-way pot. Having missed the flop entirely with no draws, I have no reason to bet. I will check to see what the original pre-flop raiser (Elon) does and re-evaluate, though I am likely folding if there is any significant action.

On the flop of 7h5sAc, I have top pair top kicker (AhTh for Ac pair with Ah kicker) on a relatively dry board with some straight draw possibilities (e.g., 68, 46) but no strong flush draws yet. Both opponents checked, signaling weakness, especially from the blinds who defended wide preflop against my button raise. With position, a deep effective stack (~7200), and pot of 900, betting for value and protection against draws is standard. Betting 2/3 pot (600) balances protection, value from worse pairs/draws, and fold equity while keeping pot manageable if raised. Opponents have shown calling tendencies in history (e.g., Minni calls multi streets light, Flash folds to aggression often), so this size extracts value without overbetting.

Board A♣ 7♥ 5♠; I checked the flop intending to fold to heavy aggression unless I had real equity. Elon bets 600 into a 1500 pot — calling 600 would make the pot 2100, so I need ≈28.6% equity to breakeven. I have J♠4♠: a strong 4‑card spade flush draw (10 spade outs) and 6 extra pair outs (3 jacks + 3 fours). Naively that's 16 outs (≈57% to hit something by river), and the pure flush chance by the river is ≈38%. Some pair outs will still be behind an Ace, so realistic equity vs a c‑bet range is ≈35–45%, which beats the ~28.6% pot‑odds. Stack is deep enough to realize implied odds if I hit. Plan: call the 600 to see the turn, fold to a sizable raise/check‑raise, and continue only if I hit a spade or pair (or pick up a clear read); if I hit the flush on the turn I can start extracting value, if I just pair a Jack proceed cautiously versus Ace heavy ranges.

I have Jack-high with no draws on an Ace-high board. Elon (the pre-flop raiser) has bet into two players, and Minni has called that bet. In a multi-way pot where there is both a bet and a call on an Ace-high texture, my Jack-high has zero equity and no path to winning the hand without a massive, ill-advised bluff. I will fold and preserve my stack.

Turn (3♦) bricks — I didn’t pair and haven’t picked up a real draw. I called the small flop c‑bet to realise a slim amount of equity, but the turn leaves me with only a backdoor possibility and very little showdown value. Elon checked the turn, which looks like pot‑control with a made hand or a probe to induce bluffs. Out of position with J♠4♠, my best play is to keep the pot small and avoid turning this marginal hand into a big bluff; I’ll check and fold to meaningful aggression on the turn or river, only continuing if I pair or pick up a clear draw or a very cheap price to see the river.

On the turn, board is 7h5sAc3d. I hold AhTh for top pair top kicker (aces with ace kicker), plus a nut flush draw in hearts (3 hearts total so far). The board is drawy with spade flush draw and possible straight draws (e.g., 46). Minni checked flop after calling my c-bet and checked again turn, showing weakness despite her history of aggression. With position, deep stacks (mine 6600 effective), and pot of 2100, betting for thin value from worse aces/pairs/draws while protecting against turn calls. Size of 1400 (~2/3 pot) continues pressure, builds pot with my equity edge, and has fold equity vs. her marginal hands. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or letting her realize equity cheaply. Opponent tendencies: Minni calls light but folds to repeated aggression post-flop in history (e.g., Hands 2,14,17). Maximizes EV over tournament.

Board: A♣ 7♥ 5♠ 3♦. I hold J♠4♠. Pot = 3500, to call = 1400 (min-raise to 2800). On the turn my backdoor spade draw is dead (turn not a spade). My only realistic outs are 3 Jacks + 3 Fours = 6 outs → ~6/46 ≈ 13.0% to hit the river. Calling would require ~28.6% equity (1400 / (3500+1400)). 13% << 28.6% and many of those outs are non-clean vs an Ace-heavy value range. Elon’s line (preflop raise, flop c-bet 600, turn bet 1400 after my check) is consistent with Ax or other strong hands, so I have very little fold equity and poor odds to continue. A bluff-raise is too risky out of position and would bloat the pot with a marginal hand. Decision: preserve chips and fold.

AK offsuit on the button vs SB and BB is a premium hand — raise for value and to take the initiative in position. I want to avoid a cheap multiway pot and isolate the blinds, so I’ll open to a standard 3x BB (300). Size gives solid fold equity vs the small blind while leaving room to maneuver post-flop and plenty of stack behind if I face a 3-bet. If I get 3-bet, I’ll re-evaluate based on who and sizing (call vs wide/aggressive 3-bets, 4-bet vs polarizing/tight lines).

I am in the Small Blind with King-Seven offsuit. Minni has raised to 3x from the button. While K7o has some high-card value in a three-handed game, playing it out of position against a deep-stacked, aggressive opponent like Minni is likely to lead to difficult post-flop situations. Folding here preserves my stack for better opportunities where I have either a stronger hand or positional advantage.

6d2c is a complete trash hand with no high card value, no suitedness, poor connectivity, and terrible playability out of position. Facing a button raise from Minni, who opens wide but my equity is minimal against her range (~20-30% of hands). Pot odds are ~2.25:1 effective (200 to call into 450), but postflop OOP with this hand is a disaster. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots. No history this hand suggests otherwise.

I am on the button in a 3-handed game with T9o. This is a standard opening hand from the button to apply pressure on the blinds and utilize my positional advantage. Looking at my previous play, a 3x open (300 chips) has been my consistent sizing for button raises.

AQo is a strong premium hand from the small blind facing a button raise. Flash has been aggressive and raises frequently from late position with a wide range. A 3-bet is standard here to isolate his wide range, build the pot for value when ahead, and take the initiative. Calling OOP risks playing out of position multiway against Minni who is deep. Raising to 900 (3x the open) is a good size given stack depths (85bb effective). Folding is too tight preflop.

Fold. My hand (Td6c) is a weak, poorly-connected offsuit holding with little postflop playability or blocker value. I’m facing a small‑blind 3‑bet after the button opened — that range is value heavy (broadways, pairs, Axs) and will dominate or have good equity vs T6o. Pot odds to call 800 into a 1,300 pot require ~27–28% equity just to break even, and the spot can easily become multiway if the button still acts, which further reduces my equity and maneuverability. With deep stacks and positional disadvantage, it’s correct to preserve chips and wait for a better spot rather than defend marginally here.

I opened from the button with T9o, a standard steal attempt. Elon responded with a 3-bet to 900 from the small blind. While I have the advantage of being in position for the rest of the hand, T9o is simply too weak to defend against a 3-bet. It lacks the connectivity of a suited hand and doesn't have enough raw equity against a standard 3-betting range. I will fold and preserve my stack.

3-handed on the button with Js8h, a speculative hand with good position and playability. Pot is 150, to call 100 for great odds, but raising is standard here to take initiative, potentially steal blinds or build pot in position. Opponents have large stacks; Minni aggressive from history, but button is powerful. Raising to 3x the BB (300 total) balances range. Folding too tight pre-flop.

I'm in the small blind with 9♦7♦ facing a button open to 300 (pot 450, 250 to call). 9-7 suited is a playable, speculative hand with deep effective stacks (I'm 216bb; opponents ~87–92bb). Calling keeps the pot manageable and realizes our equity against a wide BTN opening range; the BB may fold or call which helps our implied odds. I don't want to 3-bet with this marginal hand out of position and risk playing a bloated pot OOP. Folding is too tight given the hand's playability and stack depths. Plan: call to see the flop, continue with strong pairs or draws, fold to heavy aggression when we miss.

I have pocket Tens, which is a premium starting hand, especially in a three-handed game. Elon raised from the button (a position often played with a wide range) and Minni called from the small blind. I should raise here for value and to take initiative. A raise to 1100 chips (an 800 increase) is a standard squeeze play that builds the pot and forces my opponents to define their ranges.

3-handed on the button with J8s, a marginal speculative hand. Preflop I raised for initiative and position, but Flash's 3-bet to 1100 after Minni's flat indicates strength or a squeeze. Calling 800 into 1700 pot (~4.2:1) commits us multiway to a hand with poor equity vs 3-bet ranges (Flash likely QQ+, AK, or bluffs but history shows aggression with value). Postflop playability suffers OOP from Flash and vs Minni. 4-betting bluff risky with deep stacks and Minni's flat range. Folding preserves stack for superior spots; ~4.7bb not worth the risk long-term.

Call (total commit 1100). 9♦7♦ is a speculative, playable hand and Flash’s 3-bet to 1100 is relatively small vs our deep stacks — his range can include plenty of bluffs and suited connectors. I don’t want to 3‑bet out of position and inflate the pot; calling keeps the pot manageable and lets me realize equity with flush/straight potential. Plan: continue postflop with pairs or strong diamond/straight draws, fold to heavy aggression if I completely miss, and fold to a clear 4‑bet.

I have 9♦7♦ on K♥ T♦ 5♥ — I completely missed. There’s only a runner‑runner diamond backdoor and no pair or immediate straight draw. Flash was the preflop 3‑bettor, so his continuing range is weighted to K/T/overpairs and strong broadways; I’m out of position and shouldn’t bloat the pot with nothing. Pot is already sizeable (2,500) and I can fold to aggression and wait for a better spot or a favourable turn card (a diamond or a low card that can be credibly barreled). Action: check and give up to any meaningful bet.

I have flopped a set of Tens, which is an extremely strong hand. The board contains a King and two hearts, meaning there are several draws (straight and flush) that Minni might be holding, as well as top-pair hands like AK or KQ. Since I was the pre-flop aggressor and the pot is already significant (2500), I need to bet for value and to protect against potential draws. A bet of 1250 (50% of the pot) is large enough to build the pot and charge draws while remaining small enough to be called by a King.

I completely missed the K♥ T♦ 5♥ flop — no pair and only a backdoor diamond draw (needs runner-runner) with very little real equity. Flash was the preflop 3‑better so his continuation range on this board is heavy with Kx, Tx, overpairs and strong broadways; there’s little in that range I’m ahead of. He’s bet 1,250 into a 3,750 pot (I’m getting ~25% pot odds), but my realistic equity with 9♦7♦ is well below that. Out of position against the 3‑bettor I won’t bloat the pot with a mediocre hand — preserve chips and fold.

I’m on the button with 9♥4♠, blinds 50/100, pot 150 and it’s ~100 to see the flop (min‑raise to 200). 9‑4 offsuit has essentially no showdown or postflop playability against two defenders. Calling 100 requires roughly 40% equity to break even vs the current pot size, which this hand doesn’t have vs the wide, competent defending ranges I’ve observed from both SB (Flash) and BB (Elon). A min‑raise to try and steal is also poor — both opponents defend and 3‑bet enough that I’d just be playing a weak hand out of position in bloated pots. With deep stacks there’s no need to gamble marginal chips here, so I fold and wait for a better spot on the button.

I am in the Small Blind with 8c 2h, which is an extremely weak hand. Minni has already folded on the button. While I only need to commit 50 more chips to see the flop, playing 82o out of position against Elon is not profitable in the long run. I will fold and wait for a better spot to utilize my stack.

I am on the button with King-Trey offsuit. In a three-handed game, a King is often strong enough to open for a raise to steal the blinds. However, Minni (the Big Blind) has shown a tendency to be quite sticky, calling raises frequently and defending her big blind aggressively. King-Trey offsuit is at the bottom of a standard opening range and performs poorly post-flop against a wide defending range because of its weak kicker. Given that I have a comfortable stack and Minni is the chip leader, I will choose the low-variance path and fold this hand rather than risk chips on a marginal steal attempt.

Folded to me in the small blind with T6o. This is a profitable spot to raise wide against the big blind as part of a standard SB vs BB strategy. T6o is in the bottom of the raising range (around 40-50% of hands), offering straight potential, decent equity realization when called, and the ability to steal the dead money (150 in pot). Out of position post-flop, but Minni defends wide based on history (frequent calls), so we can continue profitably with range advantage. Minni stacks deep (204BB), so no fold equity issues; we fold to 3bets. Raising small preserves stack and maximizes EV in tournament setting. Size to 225 total (standard ~2.25x BB), consistent with prior actions (e.g., Hand 9 with similar hand). Avoid limp (poor OOP), fold too tight (misses value).

Facing Elon’s raise to 225 from the small blind with K♦5♣ in the big blind. A call costs 125 more (225 total) for ~28% pot odds — Kx heads-up has reasonable equity vs a typical SB opening range and is worth defending. Elon has shown aggression preflop, so 3-betting K5o out of position would be a thin bluff and unnecessarily bloats the pot. I’ll call to see a flop, keep the pot controllable, and proceed cautiously postflop (look to fold to heavy pressure on dry boards, or continue only if I improve or get favorable action).

Preflop SB raise with T6o was a standard steal into the BB, but this As Qh 9d flop is disastrous for my range. I have no pair, no straight draw (10 is too high for gutshot without J, no flush draw), just ten-high. Minni's calling range from BB includes Ax, KQ, QJ, suited broadways, pairs, suited connectors that connect well here (e.g., Q9, A9s, QTs). The board is coordinated enough for her value but dry for bluffs. OOP with no equity, checking preserves stack. Any bet from her forces a fold anyway, so no need to bluff without fold equity or blockers. History shows Minni bets her strong hands (e.g., Hand 14,17); she'll likely bet here. Goal is long-term stack maximization over 150 hands; folding weak spots avoids spew.

Elon checked the flop. With K♦5♣ on A♠ Q♥ 9♦ I have only K‑high and essentially no realistic draw (no backdoor flush or meaningful straight draw — only runner‑runner outs). Versus a typical SB open/raise range he’s usually ahead (A/Q/AX, broadways, pairs) so betting here is a thin bluff with low fold equity and just bloats the pot. Pot is 450 and stacks are deep enough to preserve chips — I’ll check to see the turn and keep the pot controllable. Plan: fold to any sizable bet on the turn, call very small c‑bets only for price, and become aggressive only if I pair my King or the board runs out favorably.

Preflop SB raise with T6o got called by BB Minni, flop As Qh 9d was terrible for my wide stealing range - checked through. Turn Ah pairs the aces, making the board A A Q 9 with two hearts, even worse for my range (no pair, no straight draw, no flush draw, just ten-high). Minni's check-call range from BB hits this hard: any ace (now trips possible), Qx, 9x, sets. Her flop check suggests medium strength or trap, but no equity for me to bluff profitably OOP on paired board with river to come. Pot small (450), stacks deep - betting air risks spew, low fold equity vs her range (many Ax will call/raise). Checking realizes 0 equity safely, takes free river card; fold to any bet. Long-term stack max prioritizes discipline over marginal bluffs on bad boards.

Board is 9♦ Q♥ A♠ A♥ and I hold K♦5♣ — no pair, no meaningful draw. Elon (preflop raiser) checked flop and turn; bluffing into his likely range (Ax, Qx, broadways, pairs) has very low fold equity and just bloats a 450-chip pot when I’m usually behind. Sticking to my prior plan to keep the pot controllable and avoid unnecessary bluffs: check and reevaluate on the river — fold to any meaningful bet on this street, only continue if I pair or get a very favorable card.

Preflop SB raise with T6o got checked through flop (A-high bad for range) and turn (pairs Aces, even worse). River Qd pairs Queens, creating massive value hands in Minni's BB calling range: AQ (full house), AQs (quads possible but unlikely), QQ (full house), Q9 (two pair), AA (full), trips improved, etc. My hand is pure air - ten-high, no showdown value even. Pot tiny (450) relative to deep stacks (85BB/203BB), but betting/raising air OOP on this terrifying river board has near-zero fold equity; Minni traps/checks medium-strong flop/turn and bombs river with nuts, or calls light with pairs from history (e.g., Hand 51 calls big). Bluffing spews chips long-term; checking realizes 0 equity safely, may win if she has missed draw/medium pair and checks back (her occasional passive play, e.g., Hand 54). Discipline preserves stack for better spots in tournament grind.

Board is A♠ A♥ Q♥ Q♦ 9♦ and I hold K♦5♣ — no pair, no draw. Elon was the preflop raiser and has checked flop, turn and now river. That line can be either a slowplayed full house (any A or Q makes a full house) or a pot‑control/weak holding, but either way the majority of hands that beat me will call a river bluff. Pot is only 450 and stacks are deep, so bluffing here has very low fold equity and is high variance. I have essentially zero showdown value and no good reason to inflate the pot. Best play is to check and conserve chips, folding to any meaningful bet.

AJo is a premium hand 3-handed from the button, strong equity and playability. Position allows control postflop. Pot has 150 (blinds), to call 100 into dead money suggests SB likely folded or limped minimally. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) builds pot for value, applies pressure on BB, and takes initiative. Standard sizing deepstacked. Opponents deep but history shows aggressive play; this hand dominates many calling ranges.

I have pocket Aces in the small blind facing a button open to 300. This is the best hand preflop — I should 3-bet for value and to isolate the raiser while still leaving room to play postflop. With deep effective stacks (I ~207 BB, Elon ~82 BB, Flash ~106 BB) a 3-4x sizing is appropriate: big enough to get weaker hands and many speculative callers to fold, but not so large I commit my stack unnecessarily. Raising to 1200 (total) achieves that balance — extracts value vs AK/QQ–JJ and medium pairs, reduces multiway pots, and preserves maneuverability if I face a 4-bet. Plan: call/4-bet jam or call a small 4-bet depending on villain sizing; proceed aggressively postflop if called.

I am holding 72 offsuit, which is the weakest possible starting hand in Texas Hold'em. Given that there has been an opening raise from the button and a significant 3-bet from the small blind, there is no strategic justification for continuing. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

AJo is a strong hand 3-handed on the button vs an SB 3-bet. Minni's 3-bet sizing (to 1200 over my 300 raise) is reasonably large but SB ranges 3-handed can still include a wide variety of Ax, broadway, pockets that AJo has excellent equity against (dominates AQo-ATo, KQo, etc.). Position postflop is a massive advantage against SB. Pot odds are excellent: 1600 in pot to call 900 (~1.8:1 direct, better with dead money). Calling keeps pot smaller, realizes equity multi-street IP, and avoids bloating with a 4-bet where she might fold worse or shove better. History shows Minni aggressive pre but calls/continues with medium strength; we crush bluffs/worse Ax. Folding too tight; 4-bet risks commitment deepstacked (SPR post-4bet ~3-4). Call balances range, extracts value from worse.

Flop is 3s-5d-8h (dry, rainbow). I hold Ac Ah — an overpair and almost always the best hand vs Elon's cold-call range (AK/AQ, medium pairs TT–66, occasional suited connectors). I 3-bet preflop to isolate and should now take the initiative: a value/protection c-bet denies free cards and charges worse pairs and broadways. Pot is 2500; I’ll bet a modest ~40% pot (1000) to get called by worse hands while keeping the pot controllable with deep stacks. Plan: if called, continue extracting thin value on safe turns; if Elon raises big, re-evaluate — a large check-raise is usually a set/two-pair and will force cautious action, but I’ll call reasonable pressure with AA and fold only to extremely polarized overbets if the read is strong.

AJo is a decent but not premium hand OOP vs SB 3-bet preflop, but we called IP for good pot odds (~22% equity needed vs range, have more), position, and playability. Flop 3s5d8h is rainbow, uncoordinated; hits few strong 3-bet hands (sets unlikely ~2%, TP rare). Range: QQ+/AK/AQ/sets; AJo blocks AJ/AA, has ~28-35% equity (vs QQ-KK ~30% w/ live cards/A outs, dominates AQ/KQ ~70%, folds to AK live sometimes). No pair/draw but ace-high bluff-catcher IP. Her 1000 bet (~40% pot) standard agg c-bet; likely bets all overpairs/overcards. Pot odds: 1000 into 3500 (~3.5:1, ~22% equity direct + ID on deep stacks). Call realizes equity multi-street IP, SPR post-call ~4500 pot / 6300 eff ~0.7- low commitment but her deep (18k), can pay off worse. Fold too tight 3-handed IP; raise bluff thin (no FE vs tight range, commits 2000+ into 5500 pot). History: Minni agg pre/post, but calls/continues medium S/T; we crush bluffs/worse Ax. Call balances, extracts from worse IP.

I check. I hold AcAh and opened/CB’d earlier; the pot is 4,500 after my flop bet was called. The turn (3h) pairs the board and materially increases the combos that now beat me (full houses/trips from hands containing a 5 and/or a 3, plus some combinations like pocket 55 or 3+5). Elon’s line (open, call 3‑bet, call flop) is consistent with medium pairs (88, 66), some 8x/A8/K8, and the occasional hand that now has turned very strong. Betting here risks getting check‑raised or jammed by a polarized value range and committing too many chips when I may already be behind. With deep effective stacks and a shorter opponent (~63 BB), pot control is the prudent, +EV line: check to see his action. Plan: if he checks back I’ll re-evaluate the river for a value or control bet; if he bets a small/medium size (≈≤½ pot) I’ll call for value against worse pairs; if he check‑raises large or jams I’ll fold to heavy pressure because that line is usually full house/trips and my aces are in a tough spot.

AdJs is ace-high on a paired board (3s5d8h3h) after calling flop c-bet IP vs SB 3-bet range. Her check after leading flop suggests weakness (overpair slowing down, missed draw, or air), though possible trap with set/two-pair. Board pairs low end, improving some straight draws/gutshots but not strongly favoring villains; hearts bring backdoor flush. No improvement for us, but blocks AA/AJ/strong Ax. Pot 4500, my stack 6325 (SPR ~1.4), checking realizes equity cheaply OOP for her, pot controls marginal holding, induces river bluffs/shoves from worse while protecting vs jam. Betting thin value risks commitment to low SPR spot vs calling range (QQ+/sets crush us) or folding out worse; bluffs unnecessary as check represents strength IP. History shows Minni aggressive but checks back weak hands; showdown value decent 3-handed. Check balances range after flop call.

I have Ac Ah and we 3-bet preflop, c-bet the dry flop and got called, then both checked the turn. The river 8d makes the board 3-3-8-8-5 (double-paired). That drastically reduces the value of my aces: any hand with a 3 or an 8 (single or pocket), or pocket 55, or combos like 3+5/3+8 now have full houses and beat me. Many of Elon's plausible flop-calling hands (8x, 3x, 55) are exactly the combos that turned into full houses. Betting the river for thin value is unlikely to get called by many worse hands and instead risks getting check-raised or jammed by a polarized value range. Pot control is the correct, +EV line here: check to showdown. If Elon checks back we get to see a cheap showdown and can scoop vs missed hands. If he bets a small probe (roughly <= ~1,000–1,500) I will be willing to call because of pot odds and the chance he is bluffing or barreling missed broadways; if he fires a large bet or jams (>= ~2,000–2,500) that is highly polarized toward full houses and I will fold. For now: check.

River paired board (8d pairs 8h, 3s already paired on turn) completes straight draws (46,67) and flush draws (hearts backdoor, but not really), but AdJs remains ace-high bluff-catcher with no improvement. Minni's line: 3-bet pre, bet flop (connected board), check turn/river suggests overpair slowing down fearing sets/straights, missed draw, or air checking back. Her check after leading flop indicates weakness, unlikely nuts (sets, two-pair 58/35/53 unlikely to check twice). Position IP allows check-behind for pot control/SDV with 4500 pot and 6325 stack (SPR~1.4); betting thin value (e.g., 2-2.5x pot ~2000-3000) risks folding out worse (KQ, AQ, overcards, bluffs) while facing calls/shoves from QQ+/sets/two-pair/straights we lose to. We block AA/AJ/ADx, beat her polarized weak checking range 3-handed. History: Minni aggressive but checks marginal/weak postflop. Check balances range after flop call, realizes equity cheaply, avoids commitment low-SPR. Opponent deep (18k), but showdown maximizes EV vs her calling station tendencies seen in prior hands (e.g., calls down medium strength).

Button with J♦5♥ — marginal but playable in position against the blinds. Pot is small (150) and standard open-raise sizing at this table is ~300; raising gives strong fold equity vs the SB and BB and lets me play the flop in position. My stack (23k) is deep enough to pressure Elon (6.2k) and Flash (10.5k) without committing too much. Plan: raise to a standard 300 to pick up the blinds or isolate; if I face a large 3-bet I’ll fold (J5o is not a calling 3-bet hand vs heavy aggression), if called I’ll C-bet favorable, exploit postflop position, and fold to strong resistance on highly connected/overcard boards. Raise to 300 is the best risk/reward here.

I have been dealt Pocket Aces, the strongest starting hand in Texas Hold'em. My goal is to build a large pot and maximize value. Minni has opened from the button for 300. I will 3-bet to 1100 to extract value and ideally isolate Minni or get Elon to commit his shorter stack. This sizing is standard for being out of position in the Small Blind.

9h4d is an extremely weak offsuit hand with no high card value, connectivity, or suited potential. Facing a button open to 300 and a small blind 3-bet to 1100, the ranges are strong. Calling 1000 more into a 1500 pot offers poor implied odds and no equity realization. Folding preserves my stack for better spots in this tournament phase.

I opened to 300 as planned and Flash (SB) 3-bet to 1,100; Elon folded so it’s heads-up. J♦5♥ is a weak, largely dominated hand vs a typical SB 3-bet range (lots of Ax, broadways and pairs) and has poor postflop playability. Calling 800 to win a ~2,300 pot requires ~35% equity — J5o is unlikely to have that vs a sane 3-bet range. A small 4-bet bluff (min to 1,900) is unattractive: it’s not enough to credibly fold out better hands and will get called or 5-bet too often. With a deep stack and many better spots ahead, folding and preserving chips is the +EV choice here.

I am on the button with 72o, the worst starting hand in poker. Although I am in position, there is no strategic reason to play this hand given its extremely poor equity and post-flop playability. Folding is the only sensible move.

Preflop in the small blind facing a folded button, so heads-up versus Minni in the big blind. My hand Qc6h is offsuit, queen-high with mediocre playability but decent blocker value. Standard strategy in SB vs BB unopened is to complete very wide due to favorable pot odds (calling 50 more into 150 pot for immediate odds, plus postflop position advantage). Equity against a typical BB defending range is around 40-50%, and I can realize equity well in position postflop. Folding is too tight; raising adds unnecessary risk with a marginal hand against an aggressive Minni who defends wide. Completing keeps the pot small and exploits the positional edge to maximize stack growth over many hands.

Heads-up vs the SB who limped. Tc7h is a marginal but playable hand in this spot — heads-up it has decent equity and playability. I want to take the initiative and isolate the limp rather than give Elon free flop chances, so I raise a small/isolation raise to 300 total (add 200 to my posted 100). This sizing preserves pot control against his ~61BB stack, gives fold equity, and lets me c-bet many flops. If he 3-bets big I'll respect strength and can fold; if he calls I can continue with positional advantage on many boards.

Preflop in SB, I limped with Qc6h, a marginal queen-high offsuit hand playable for position in unopened pot. However, facing Minni's raise to 300 (3x the BB after my limp), this is a standard fold. Her BB raising range after SB limp is strong: pairs 66+, broadway combos, suited aces, suited connectors. Q6o has ~30-35% equity against that, poor playability OOP (SB acts first postflop), and doesn't block her value well. Calling 200 into ~600 pot offers ~3:1 odds but requires too much postflop equity realization, which is difficult with reverse position and weak hand. Raising as a bluff is -EV given her wide defending range and deep stacks (61BB). Folding preserves stack for better spots to maximize long-term EV over 150 hands.

AQ offsuit is a premium hand from the button in a 3-handed pot. Excellent position for post-flop play. Opponents are deep-stacked, but raising standard size builds the pot with position while denying equity to weaker hands. Minni and Flash have shown aggression in history, but AQ dominates their calling ranges like suited connectors or lower pairs. Raising to 3x the big blind (300) is optimal for value and isolation. Stack depth supports this without overcommitting.

QTo is a defendable hand from the small blind vs a button open in a 3-handed game. Pot is 450, I’ve posted 50 and need 250 more to call (total 300). With deep stacks I want to realize equity and see a flop rather than make a thin, small 3-bet (min-raising to 500 would show little strength and bloat a pot out of position). Elon’s opens are often wide from the button, so calling keeps his range wide and lets me play flops where I can hit top pair, two-pair, or straight/runner-runner possibilities. Plan: call, see flop; continue with top pair/good draws, be cautious OOP without strong equity, fold to heavy action on dry boards.

I am in the Big Blind with Ten-Six of clubs. Elon raised from the button to 300, and Minni called from the Small Blind. The pot is currently 700, and it costs me 200 to call. This gives me pot odds of 3.5 to 1. Given that I am closing the pre-flop action in a multi-way pot with a suited hand that has some connectivity, this is a standard call to see a flop and attempt to realize equity.

Flop Qd 6d Ac; I hold QhTd — top pair with a medium kicker. This is a multiway, out-of-position spot against two opponents (Elon opened wide from the button; Flash defends the BB wide), so many hands in their continuing ranges beat me (Ax, sets) and several have diamond draws. Betting here has limited fold equity and will bloat the pot when I’m often behind. I’d rather control the pot size, see how opponents react, and realize equity. Plan: check to keep the pot manageable. If I face a small-to-medium bet (≈≤1/2 pot ≈450) I’ll call to keep worse Qx and draws in; if I face a large overbet or a check-raise I’ll fold most of the time since I’m usually second-best. If both check back I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and look to extract thin value on safe runouts or continue pot-control on dangerous diamond/ace turns.

I have bottom pair (sixes) on a board of A-Q-6. This board texture is very favorable for the pre-flop raiser's range (Elon), and since I am out of position in a three-way pot, leading out with a weak pair is not advisable. Checking is the standard play here to see how the action develops behind me.

Top two pair (Aces and Queens) on a Qd-6d-Ac flop is an extremely strong hand, flopped the nuts practically except for sets or two pair better like AQ suited or AT. I hold the Ad, blocking nut flush draws. Both opponents checked, suggesting weakness or caution due to the coordinated board with diamond draw and possible straight draws, but my hand dominates most of their ranges. Minni and Flash have shown aggression historically but checking here likely indicates capped ranges. Betting 2/3 pot (600 into 900) extracts value from weaker pairs, top pair, draws (flush, gutshots like 75/54), and floats while building the pot with position. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or allowing bluffs. Deep stacks support this sizing without overexposure.

Elon bets 600 into a 900 pot (pot now 1500). Calling 600 means I need roughly 600/(1500+600) ≈ 28.6% equity to break even. I have top pair (Q) with a decent ten kicker — a hand that fares reasonably well against a wide button c-bet range that includes lots of A-highs, Qx and diamond semi-bluffs. Raising out of position would bloat the pot and polarize my range with a marginal hand; folding feels too nitty with top pair in a multiway spot. I will call to realize equity and see a turn, plan to fold to heavy turn/river aggression on obvious A or diamond runouts or a check-raise line, and try to extract thin value on safe runouts.

I have bottom pair (sixes) on a board that heavily favors the pre-flop raiser (Elon) and the caller (Minni). The board is Ace-Queen-Six with two diamonds. Elon bet 600 into 900, which is a strong continuation bet into two players, and Minni called. My bottom pair is very vulnerable and likely already beaten by a better pair (Aces or Queens) or a draw. Given the action from both opponents on this texture, there is no profitable way to continue with such a weak hand out of position. I will fold to preserve my stack.

Heads-up on the turn (pot 2100) with Qd 6d Ac 8h and QhTd in my hand: I have top pair with a medium kicker that beats most bluffs and worse Qx but loses to any A. Elon is a wide, aggressive button opener who c-bet the flop, so his continuing range contains many Ax, some Qx, and diamond semi-bluffs. Betting a little under/around half-pot (~1050) is the right balance: it extracts thin value from worse Qx/KQ and charges any remaining diamond backdoors, while keeping the pot controllable OOP. If Elon mucks or calls, I can re-evaluate on the river and extract more on safe cards; if he raises large, I’ll fold most of the time (I’m usually behind to a raise).

Top two pair (AQ) on Qd6dAc8h remains a monster, dominating Minni's likely check-call flop then turn bet range: weaker Ax (AJ-AT, A-rag), Qx (QJ-QT suited), pocket pairs like 77-55, or missed diamond draws turning into value/thin value. A8 two pair or sets possible but low frequency given preflop action (SB call vs BTN raise) and check flop. Ad blocks nut flush draw, Qc blocks str8 draws. Her 1/2 pot turn lead after check-call suggests medium strength trying to charge draws or take it away, but my hand crushes that. Raising to 2500 (minraise + value, ~3/4 pot effective) extracts max value from worse while keeping SPR manageable (~5225-2500=2725 into ~5650 pot). Position allows control; if she shoves (possible A8/set), can call off profitably as equity vs nuts ~30% but range weighted worse. Calling too passive, risks cheap river; fold insane.

I raised the turn (1050) as a thin value / probing bet to charge draws and get value from worse Qx. Elon’s immediate reraise to 2500 is basically an all‑in (he has ~2725 to start, leaves ~225) and polarizes his range strongly toward value (Ax, sets, two‑pair combos, maybe a few bluffs). The pot is 5650 and I must call 1450 more, so I need ~1450/(5650+1450) ≈ 20.4% equity to break even. With Q♣T♦ (top pair, medium kicker) I have only about 5 clean outs to beat a pure A‑high (3 tens + 2 queens) → ~5/46 ≈ 10.9% equity on the river, and vs sets/two‑pair my equity is even worse. Given the sizing, opponent profile and my earlier plan to fold to a large raise, this is a negative‑EV call. Preserve the deep stack and let Elon take this one — fold.

On the Button with 3♦9♥ — a weak hand, but deep stacks (≈209bb) and only the blinds to act behind make this a good spot to open-steal. I want to take the initiative and use position rather than limp and play a passive pot. I’ll raise to 300 (3bb): big enough to apply pressure and avoid giving a free flop, but small enough to fold to a sizable 3-bet without risking much. Plan: fold to a significant 3-bet, but if one or both blinds call I'll use position to c-bet on dry flops and play cautiously on dangerous boards.

I am in the Small Blind with Queen-Seven offsuit (Q7o). The Button (Minni) has raised to 3x the big blind. While Q7o has some high-card value in a three-handed game, it plays very poorly out of position and is generally not strong enough to call a raise or 3-bet as a bluff. Given the stack sizes and the potential for the Big Blind (Elon) to squeeze or defend, folding is the most disciplined and standard play to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

In the big blind facing a button raise to 300 (3x the BB), my hand Kc5s is offsuit king-high with a weak kicker and poor playability post-flop. Button ranges are wide, but K5o doesn't make strong hands often and struggles against typical calling/3-betting ranges. Pot odds are 450:200 (~2.25:1, requiring ~31% equity), but my equity vs. a wide button open (~40% maybe) is offset by reverse implied odds and position disadvantage. Minni has been aggressive historically, often raising light, but calling here bloats the pot OOP with a marginal hand. Better to fold and wait for stronger spots to maximize stack long-term in this multi-hand tournament.

In a three-handed game, 6h 7s is a marginal hand from the button. While it is a connector, being offsuit significantly reduces its value. Given that the big stack (Minni) is in the Big Blind and has shown a high frequency of defending and aggressive post-flop play, I choose to fold and wait for a stronger hand to avoid playing a bloated pot out of position or against a sticky opponent with a weak holding.

SB vs BB after button fold is a good stealing spot. K6o is a decent hand with king blocker, top ~30% range. Raising to 3x BB (300) puts pressure on Minni, who has shown aggression but also folds to raises in history (e.g., multiple folds pre/post). OOP postflop ok if we take it down now. Folding too tight early in tournament with deep stacks; limping risks multiway pots OOP with marginal hand. Pot 150, commit 250 more for potential steal of 150.

I’m in the big blind with QcTh facing a standard small‑blind open to 300. I only need to put in 200 more (total 300) to see a flop and the pot after my call will be ~600, so I’m getting ~33% pot odds. QTo is a strong, playable hand heads‑up against a likely wide SB opening range and will realize good equity postflop; I’m deep stacked so I don’t want to bloat the pot OOP with a marginal 3‑bet. Plan: call to see the flop, continue with top pair/strong draws or pot‑control/fold to heavy aggression when I miss.

Flop 9h5d2s is a dry, uncoordinated board that misses much of Minni's BB calling range against my SB steal-raise (Ax high, suited broadways, pairs, suited connectors). No pair or draw with K6o, but king-high has decent showdown value and blocks Kx. As OOP aggressor, a standard c-bet exploits her wide range, denies equity to floaters/draws, and takes down the pot ~60-70% of the time given history of her folding to postflop aggression (e.g., Hand 53, 55, 77). Pot control less important with deep stacks; bet 1/3-1/2 pot sizes well (300 into 600). If called, evaluate turn with fold equity. Checking risks giving free cards and playing OOP multi-street pots with marginal hand.

I’m OOP in the big blind with QcTh on a very dry 9h-5d-2s flop. Elon opened wide from the SB and his small 300 c-bet into the ~900 pot is standard. Calling 300 gives me ~25% pot odds and QTo has decent equity vs a wide continuation range plus strong implied odds with the stacks we have. I don’t want to inflate the pot out of position with a bluff‑3bet — the correct line is to call and see a turn. Plan: continue (bet/raise) if I pair a Q or T or pick up meaningful equity; fold to heavy turn aggression if I miss and have no improvement.

Turn pairs my 6d perfectly for middle pair with a strong king kicker, improving from complete air to a solid hand with showdown value. Board 9h5d2s6c remains relatively dry but introduces a club flush draw alongside the existing heart and spade flush draws from flop, plus gutshot possibilities (e.g., 3-4, 7-8, 10-7). Minni's flop call range vs my SB steal cbet is wide: Ax (A9, A5+), pocket pairs (22-88 likely, 99+ less), suited connectors/draws. Continued aggression as pre/flop aggressor represents overpairs, 9x, sets, or strong Ax, extracting value from her weaker pairs/draws while protecting against equity realization. Checking OOP risks giving free cards to draws or inviting bluffs/check-raises. 2/3 pot bet (800 into 1200) balances value/protection/fold equity; pot odds ~33% to call deny some floats. Deep stacks allow flexibility; if raised, king blocker helps vs 2p+, can fold to heavy action. History shows Minni calls but folds to turn aggression when behind (e.g., Hands 47,51). Goal: build stack, this spot +EV.

Turn analysis: I missed — QcTh on 9h-5d-2s-6c has no pair, no meaningful draws. I only have 6 clean outs (three Qs, three Ts) to pair on the river, ~6/46 ≈ 13% equity. The pot is 2000 and Elon bet 800; calling 800 to win 2800 requires ~28.6% equity to break even. My equity (≈13%) is far below that, so a pure call is -EV.

Range and opponent: Elon opened and c-bet the flop then barreled the turn — his range here contains many value hands (9x, middle pairs, sets, overpairs) that dominate me or are essentially drawing dead. While a check-raise bluff is possible, I'm out of position and he has shown willingness to apply pressure; a raise here will often meet a call or a shove from hands that beat me and is unnecessarily risky.

Decision: Preserve a healthy stack and pick better spots. Fold and wait for a hand where I have real equity or can effectively apply leverage OOP.

8d5s is a weak suited hand with a large gap, not strong enough to play multiway from the button in a 3-handed pot. Pot odds are decent (150:100), but post-flop playability is poor, and opponents like Minni are aggressive. Stacks are deep enough to wait for better spots to maximize EV over remaining hands. Clear fold to preserve stack.

I’m in the small blind with Qh6c — a marginal offsuit queen. The pot is 150 and I only need to put 50 more to call (total commit 100). Calling is the low-variance, +EV line here: it gets me good pot odds to see a flop, keeps the pot small and avoids building a bloated pot out of position with a weak holding. Raising as a steal is an option, but Flash (BB) has shown willingness to defend and I don’t want to face a 3-bet or play large OOP with Q6o. I’ll call and evaluate on the flop.

I have 3s 5h in the big blind. Elon has folded on the button, and Minni has completed the small blind. Since I am in the big blind and there is no raise, I can see a free flop. My hand is very weak, so there is no reason to raise and build a pot with such low equity. Checking is the standard play here.

I have Qh6c on Ac-4s-6h — second pair (sixes) with a queen kicker. I’m out of position in a small pot (200) against a BB who defends widely; their range contains a lot of Ax that currently beat me. Leading out here mainly gets paid off by Axs and bloats the pot OOP with a marginal hand, so I prefer pot control. I’ll check to see how Flash reacts: if he checks back I get to a cheap showdown with a hand that has reasonable showdown value; if he bets I can call small-to-medium c-bets to realize equity and fold to heavy aggression (which will usually be polarized to Aces/trips). With my healthy stack there’s no need to inflate the pot now.

I am in the Big Blind in a limped pot against Minni (Small Blind). The flop is Ace-high (Ac 4s 6h), and I have 5-high with a gutshot straight draw (needing a 2). Minni has checked to me. In a limped pot with such a weak hand, there is no reason to bet and risk being check-raised or called when I have very little equity and no showdown value. Checking back allows me to see the turn for free and potentially realize equity if a 2 or a helpful card appears.

I have Qh6c on Ac‑4s‑6h‑2d — a single pair (sixes) with a decent kicker. The pot is small (200) and Flash (BB) checked the flop and has shown no aggression, so his range is wide and includes many hands that either currently miss (Kx, Qx, small pairs) or have an Ace that beats me. Betting OOP with a vulnerable one‑pair hand mainly bloats the pot and invites raises from Ax or better; there are also no significant draws to charge. Pot control and showdown value are the priority: I’ll check and see how Flash acts on the river. If he bets a small/medium size I can call and realize equity; I’ll fold to clear heavy aggression that polarizes to better hands.

The turn card is the Deuce of Diamonds, which completes a six-high straight (2-3-4-5-6) with my Trey of Spades and Five of Hearts. The board is Ace-high and rainbow (Ac 4s 6h 2d), so there are no flushes possible and no higher straights can be made using the available board cards. This means I currently hold the absolute nuts. Minni has checked for the second time, and since I need to extract value from my hand, I will bet 150 into the 200 pot. This sizing is designed to look like a standard probe bet on an Ace-high board, which may induce a call from any Ace, a pair of fours or sixes, or even a gutshot draw.

I have Qh6c on Ac‑4s‑6h‑2d — a vulnerable one‑pair (sixes) with a decent kicker, out of position. Flash checked the flop and now bets a small 150 into the ~200 pot; his line contains Ax (which beats me) but also a lot of missed broadways, small pairs and probing bets. Calling 150 to win ~350 gives ~30% pot‑odds — my hand has enough showdown value against his non‑Ace range and raising OOP would needlessly bloat the pot and fold out only worse hands while getting value‑raised by Ax. I’ll call to keep the pot controlled, realize equity, and re‑evaluate the river (fold to heavy aggression, call small/medium river bets, consider a river raise only if I improve or see clear weakness).

Board: A♣ 4♠ 6♥ 2♦ 9♣. I hold Q♥6♣ — a single pair of sixes with a decent kicker, out of position. Flash checked the flop and made a small 150 bet on the turn into ~200, a line consistent with a wide range (thin Ax value, small pairs, probes or bluffs). There’s little to gain by barreling OOP with a marginal pair: a bet would bloat the pot and get called or raised by hands that beat me (Ax, better pairs) while folding out only the very weakest holdings. Checking keeps the pot controlled and lets me realize showdown value. Plan: check to the opponent and only call a small/medium river bet; fold to a large polarized shove. Action: check.

I currently hold a straight (2-3-4-5-6) which is the absolute nuts on this board (Ac 4s 6h 2d 9c), as no higher straight or flush is possible. Minni has checked to me for the third time this hand. After betting 150 on the turn and receiving a call, the pot is now 500. I need to extract maximum value from any Ace or small pair Minni might be holding. A bet of 350 (70% of the pot) is a standard value bet size that is likely to be called by a range of marginal hands in a limped pot.

Call 350. I have Q♥6♣ — one pair (sixes) with a decent kicker — out of position. Flash’s line (check on the flop, a small 150 turn bet I called, now a small 350 river bet into an 850 pot) is consistent with a wide, weak-to-medium range: thin Ax value hands, small pairs, missed broadways and probing bluffs. Calling 350 to win ~1550 gives ~22.6% pot‑odds; my hand has reasonable showdown equity vs that mixed range, so a call is the low‑variance, +EV play. Raising OOP with a vulnerable pair would bloat the pot, fold out worse hands, and get value‑raised or shoved by the Ax/strong hands I’m behind — so I’ll not inflate it. Plan: call and show down; fold to any larger raise but otherwise take the cheap showdown.

I’m on the button with A♣4♥ — a hand worth opening from position. Pot is 150 and to‑call is 100; I want to apply pressure to the blinds and avoid a limp‑multiway pot. A standard 3x BB open to 300 is consistent with my table image and gives good fold equity while leaving plenty of stack to play post‑flop in position. If I face a large 3‑bet I’ll re‑evaluate (likely fold to a big shove or 3‑bet size that commits a lot); if called I’ll c‑bet favorable textures and use position to control the pot.

I am in the Small Blind with Queen-Seven offsuit. Minni, who is currently the chip leader, has raised from the button. While I have a healthy stack, Q7o is a very weak hand to defend out of position, especially against an aggressive opponent. Folding here preserves my stack for better opportunities where I have a positional or card advantage.

In the Big Blind facing a loose button raise from Minni, who is aggressive preflop based on history (frequent raises from late position). A6o is a standard defending hand with excellent pot odds: 200 to call into 450 pot (~2.25:1 direct odds, needing ~31% equity, but A6o has ~38-42% vs typical 45% BTN range). Strong Ace blocker vs value hands. OOP but deep stacks (88BB effective for me), flop and realize equity if connects (top pair good kicker, straight potential). 3-bet bluff possible but offsuit A6 prefers calling range over polarized 3-bet spot. Folding too tight here preserves BB defense strategy to maximize EV over tournament.

Preflop defended BB vs button raise with A6o, solid hand with good pot odds and ace blocker. Flop 6s5cJd rainbow gives me bottom pair (6s) with strong Ace kicker, decent showdown value vs button's wide C-bet range (Ax, broadway, suited connectors). Board is somewhat drawy (gutshots like T7, 74; OESD potential with lower connectors), but rainbow reduces flush worries. OOP, leading weak as it caps my range and invites raises; better to check and realize equity by check-calling a standard bet (button cbets 60-70% here). Pot 650 (~13BB), deep stacks (~85BB effective), check preserves fold equity if checked through and controls pot. If oversized bet, can fold; small/medium call profitably (~33-40% equity needed, have it vs polarized range). Consistent with BB defense strategy to extract value long-term.

I was the preflop aggressor and I have position — this flop (J♦ 6♠ 5♣) misses a large portion of the BB’s calling range. A♣4♥ has decent showdown value (the Ace is a meaningful blocker) but no real draws, so I want to use my position and fold equity to take the pot down. I’ll fire a continuation bet of about half the pot to pressure J‑misses and overcards while keeping the price reasonable if called. Plan: take it down when Elon folds; if he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn (check to control the pot unless I pair up or face a cheap spot to barrel); fold to a large check‑raise since A4 has poor equity versus that line.

Bottom pair top kicker (Ad6h on 6s5cJd) is a strong hand OOP vs Button's wide c-bet range after loose preflop raise. Excellent pot odds: 350 to call into 1000 pot (~22% equity needed, but ~45-55% vs typical button cbets including Ax, broadway misses, suited connectors, pocket pairs below J). Rainbow board with limited draws (gutshots like 74/T7); my blocker to Ad reduces AJ likelihood. Deep effective stacks (~85BB), small half-pot bet doesn't warrant raise (risks folding out worse Ax/draws or facing Jx shove). Check-call realizes equity, controls pot, allows turn play (implied odds if improves, fold to big bet if behind). Consistent with BB defense strategy vs LAG button; folding too tight, raising too thin OOP. Long-term EV positive in tournament.

On turn 2h, board now 6s5cJd2h, holding Ad6h for bottom pair top kicker OOP vs aggressive Button after check-call flop. The deuce adds minor straight draws (e.g., 74 improved to gutshot, but no wheel) and opens heart flush draw, increasing Minni's bluffing range (missed draws like 97h, ATh). My equity remains strong (~45% vs her polarized range post-flop c-bet/call: Jx, sets, Ax, draws, bluffs). Pot 1350 (~13.5BB), deep effective stacks (~80BB). Leading risks folding out worse (Ax, weaker pairs/draws) and invites raises from Jx; check realizes equity by check-calling value or inducing bluffs, controls pot OOP, consistent with BB defense vs LAG. If checked through, happy to showdown; if bet, excellent odds to call small/medium. Folding/shoving too extreme. Long-term tournament EV maximized by playing post-flop spots profitably.

I opened pre‑flop and c‑bet the flop (J♦6♠5♣) and Elon called. The turn (2♥) is a blank that doesn’t improve my A♣4♥—I only have ace‑high and no real draws. Betting here will often bloat the pot against a caller’s range (Jx, mid pairs, 6/5, floats) who will call too frequently, leaving me with poor equity. In position I prefer pot control and to realize showdown value: check the turn, see Elon’s action on the river, and then decide (call small/medium river bets, fold to large river shove; consider a small river bluff if it looks like he will fold). For now: check to keep the pot manageable and retain flexibility.

River Js pairs the Jd, giving the board paired jacks (Jd Js). Holding Ad6h for two pair (JJ66 with A kicker) after check-calling flop bottom pair and checking turn. Strong vs unpaired hands (Ax, KQ, etc., now single pair JJ), but loses to any Jx (trips JJx, heavily in Minni's button raise + flop c-bet range on J-high board) or sets (55/22). No flush/straight likely (board 2h5c6sJdJs: possible straights limited/none obvious). Minni checked turn behind, capping strong hands somewhat but could trap with Jx or control pot; OOP first to act, betting risks raises from better (Jx) folding out worse (Ax), poor EV. Checking realizes equity: induces bluffs/value bets from Jx which can call profitably (~40% equity needed vs polarized betting range, have it vs Ax/draws > trips proportion); happy showdown vs single pair if checked behind. Consistent with prior BB defense vs aggressive button; preserves stack deep (~82BB) in tournament, maximizes long-term EV by avoiding OOP river jams.

I’m on the button with A♣4♥. I opened preflop, c‑bet the flop to 350 and Elon called; turn was a blank and we both checked. The river paired the Jack (board J♦ 6♠ 5♣ 2♥ J♠) and I’ve got only ace‑high. Elon’s line (call flop, check turn, check river) is highly consistent with Jx, mid pair or a slow‑played/medium hand — all of which beat me. Betting as a thin bluff has poor fold equity against a caller who’s shown willingness to call the flop; building the pot with no showdown equity is a losing line. With comfortable stacks and pot control in position the correct play is to check and show down; if Elon then barrels small I can consider a call for pot odds, but I’ll fold to a sizable value bet. Action: check to keep the pot manageable and realize showdown.

I am on the button with 8d 3s, which is a very weak hand. Although standard strategy in a 3-handed game allows for a wider opening range from the button, 83 offsuit is too low in equity to justify a raise or a limp, especially with an aggressive big blind like Minni. I will fold to conserve my stack and wait for a better spot.

Heads-up vs BB after BTN fold. KTs suited is a premium hand in this spot: high equity, suited for flush potential, plays well post-flop. Raising builds the pot for value against BB's wide defending range while taking initiative. Standard size ~3x BB (300 total). Folding SB or limping is suboptimal.

Elon opened to 300 from the small blind and I already have 100 in as the big blind, so calling costs me 200 more (300 total committed). Pot is 400 so my break-even equity on a call is ~33%. 4♦2♦ is weak but suited and has backdoor straight/flush potential; in a heads-up defense from the big blind it’s an acceptable cold-defend to a standard 3bb open, especially with deep stacks and good implied odds. I don’t want to bloat the pot or create a marginal 3‑bet spot with trash, so I’ll call and see a flop; I’ll fold to a sizable 3‑bet preflop and postflop will continue only with a diamond draw, pair, or a favorable board texture.

Preflop raiser with top pair (kings) and ten kicker on an ace-high flop with a spade flush draw. TdKd blocks strong kings like KQs/KJs and has backdoor diamond flush draw. Minni's BB calling range includes Ax, weaker Kx, suited broadways, and some suited connectors. A continuation bet protects against overcards/draws, builds pot for value against Ax/worse pairs, and denies equity to spades. OOP, but initiative favors c-betting here. 2/3 pot size (400 into 600) balances value/protection without overcommitting with marginal kicker.

Board is Ks 6s Ad and I hold 4♦2♦. Elon’s flop bet is 400 into a ~1000 pot — calling requires ~28.6% equity. My hand has no pair and only a backdoor diamond possibility (would need two running diamonds — ~4.2% to hit by river) plus small-card pair equity that often still loses to Ax/Kx. Raising as a bluff is thin (no meaningful blockers and a min-raise won’t credibly fold many of his value hands; he can also shove). Given pot odds and my very weak real equity, folding is the correct +EV play — preserve my stack and wait for a better spot with real equity or fold equity.

2h3c is an extremely weak offsuit hand with minimal potential for straights, no suited value, and weak high card strength. Even on the button in a 3-handed pot with position, the playability is poor, and entering voluntarily risks getting dominated or outdrawn postflop against likely stronger ranges from blinds. Pot odds of calling 100 into 150 are decent (~3:1), but implied odds are low due to capped range and reverse implied odds with such a weak hand. Opponents are aggressive but no need to speculate here. Folding preserves stack for premium spots in deep-stack tournament play to maximize long-term EV.

Heads-up from the small blind with 4♣6♦. Against a single opponent I should be opening a very wide range — limp/calling 50 is too passive and gives Flash free equity. I’ll make the standard min-open to 200 to take the initiative, leverage my positional advantage postflop, and apply fold equity. The raise is small relative to our deep stacks so I can continue on favorable boards or fold to heavy 3-bets; overall this maximizes expected value with minimal risk.

I am in the Big Blind with 9s Ts, a strong suited connector that performs well post-flop. Minni, in the Small Blind, has made a min-raise to 200. I only need to call 100 more to see the flop, getting 3-to-1 odds. While a 3-bet is a viable option to take control of the hand, calling allows me to play a pot in position throughout the rest of the hand with a hand that has great playability. Given the favorable price and the strength of my hand relative to a typical SB opening range, calling is the most standard and effective play here.

I missed this flop entirely. With 4♣6♦ on 9♦ J♦ T♥ I have no pair and no real draw (only a runner‑runner diamond possibility). The flop is very coordinated and heavily favors the BB’s calling range (Qx/KQ/KJ, pairs, two‑pair/sets, and many strong draws/straights). As the pre‑flop raiser I could bluff, but against a wide, call‑heavy range a c‑bet here is a thin, often unprofitable bluff. I’m in position, so I’ll check to control the pot, see what Flash does, and preserve my deep stack. If Flash gives a small probing bet I can consider a float and try to take it away on the turn; but I’ll fold to significant pressure with nothing. Checking maximizes flexibility and minimizes risk with a pure bluff on this texture.
